Exit poll results highlights: Exit poll projections for the assembly elections to four states — Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal — and the Union territory of Puducherry were released today, with a narrow win projected for the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) in Trinamool Congress bastion Bengal and a Congress-UDF return in Kerala....Read More
While voting for Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry concluded earlier, the second and last phase voting for West Bengal took place today, only after the conclusion of which were the exit poll results released, according to Election Commission of India (ECI) rules.
Along with assembly elections, assembly bypolls to eight seats across six states have also taken place. Voting for five seats - Goa (Ponda), Karnataka (Bagalkote, Devanagere), Nagaland (Koridang), and Tripura (Dharmanagar) - was held on April 9, while three seats in Gujarat (Umreth) and Maharashtra (Rahuri, Baramati) voted on April 23.
Exit poll results 2026 | Key points
-Assembly election voting: Voting for Assam, Kerala, Puducherry assembly elections happened in a single phase on April 9. Tamil Nadu also voted in one phase on April 23 along with the first phase polling for West Bengal, where the second and final leg is taking place today.
-When will results be out: Results for the assembly elections and the bypolls to eight assembly constituencies across six states will be known on May 4, the day the Election Commission counts the votes.
-Election voting time: Voting ideally takes place from 7 am and goes on till 6 pm, however, it does not end till all people in the last queues cast their franchise.
-Assembly seats: Assam has a total of 126 assembly seats, Kerala 140, Tamil Nadu 234, Bengal 294 and Puducherry 30. This makes the majority mark a party or an alliance needs to be able to form government in Assam 64, in Kerala 71, in Bengal 148 and 16 in Puducherry.
-Exit poll results time: Exit poll predictions for the ongoing election cycle are released only after the official restriction period ends, as per EC guidelines. The Election Commission has clearly defined a “silence period” during which no voter survey results can be published or broadcast. According to the rules, media organisations were barred from conducting or releasing exit polls from 7 am on April 9 to 6:30 pm on April 29.
The live updates to the blog are closed.
The live updates to the blog are closed.
Bengal exit poll results LIVE: Axis My India yet to give Bengal projection, release expected on Thursday
Bengal exit poll results LIVE: Axis My India has held back its projections for West Bengal, with the pollster expected to release the figures on Thursday.
Its projections for Kerala indicate a victory for the Congress-led alliance with 78–90 seats, while the ruling Left Democratic Front is expected to trail with 49–62 seats. The National Democratic Alliance is likely to secure 0–3 seats.
In Tamil Nadu, the pollster projected a close contest between the ruling Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam and Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, which is making its electoral debut. The DMK is expected to win 92–110 seats, while TVK could secure 98–120. The NDA, another key contender in the state, is placed third with 22–32 seats.
In Assam, the NDA is set for a comfortable win, with the BJP-led alliance projected to secure 88–100 seats, while the Congress-led front may get 24–36. All India United Democratic Front is likely to draw a blank, with others expected to win between zero and three seats.
In Puducherry, the NDA is projected to win 16–20 seats, while the DMK-Congress alliance may secure 6–8. TVK is likely to get 2–4 seats in the 30-member Assembly.
Tamil Nadu exit poll results LIVE: DMK predicts 180+ seats in Tamil Nadu as AIADMK rejects exit polls
Tamil Nadu exit poll results LIVE: With several exit polls forecasting a strong win for the ruling Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam in the recently concluded Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, the party expressed confidence of securing more than 180 seats. The state has 234 constituencies, with the majority mark set at 118.
DMK spokesperson Saravanan Annadurai said the projections were conservative and asserted that the party would comfortably cross the 180-seat mark.
Meanwhile, the opposition All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam dismissed the exit polls, maintaining that voters have already opted for a change in government in the April 23 elections.
Bengal exit poll results LIVE: BJP’s Samik Bhattacharya says TMC will be 'swept out' on counting day, takes swipe at Mamata
Bengal exit poll results LIVE: West Bengal BJP president Samik Bhattacharya on Wednesday took a swipe at Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, saying that if believing in a TMC victory helps her sleep, “there’s no need to disturb it,” while asserting that the BJP will sweep the state after counting day on May 4.
“If thinking this way helps someone sleep for two days, there’s no reason to interrupt it. Say whatever you want. Didi is going on May 4,” he told ANI.
“What is the Trinamool Congress’s culture? What is its style of functioning? If someone can surround Abhaya’s mother and raise such slogans, the entire country is watching. All of West Bengal is losing, and the whole world can see it,” he said.
Bengal exit poll results LIVE: TMC’s Shashi Panja rejects Bengal exit polls, alleges EC and central forces influenced voting
Bengal exit poll results LIVE: Responding to exit poll projections for the West Bengal Assembly elections, TMC leader Shashi Panja on Wednesday urged caution and said the final outcome would only be known on counting day. She dismissed the projections and accused the Election Commission and central forces of affecting the electoral process.
Speaking to ANI, Panja said, “We should wait till May 4. The ECI and the central government will have to take responsibility for how the elections were conducted, how emotions were stirred, and how central forces treated voters on polling day.”
Tamil Nadu exit poll results LIVE: Exit polls project tight BJP edge in Bengal, UDF comeback in Kerala
Tamil Nadu exit poll results LIVE: Exit poll projections for the assembly elections to four states - Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal - and the Union territory of Puducherry were released today, with a narrow win projected for the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) in Trinamool Congress bastion Bengal and a Congress-UDF return in Kerala.
While voting for Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry concluded earlier, the second and last phase voting for West Bengal took place today, only after the conclusion of which were the exit poll results released, according to Election Commission of India (ECI) rules.
Tamil Nadu exit poll results LIVE: Pollsters project BJP debut in Bengal, return in Assam; DMK set to retain Tamil Nadu
Tamil Nadu exit poll results LIVE: BJP government's debut in West Bengal and return in Assam as well as the Union territory of Puducherry has been projected by pollsters in the 2026 assembly elections. In Tamil Nadu, the DMK-led alliance is predicted to return to power while the Congress-led UDF is projected to topple the LDF government in Kerala.

Tamil Nadu exit poll results LIVE: The Axis My India exit poll for Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections 2026 has predicted a strikingly different picture from other projections, hinting at a fragmented verdict rather than a clear winner, with the vote leaning towards actor Vijay's party - TVK.

While most pollsters have projected 142-145 seats for the BJP, the TMC is predicted to bag 99-152 constituencies. One pollster - Janmat - has emerged to be an outlier, projecting 200-plus seats for the TMC. Another pollster, People's Pulse, has also predicted a comfortable win for the TMC with 177-187 seats.

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score, IPL 2026: Second half of Super Sunday takes us back to the foot of the IPL 2026 table. It has been a campaign to forget for KKR and LSG alike thus far – Kolkata finally got their first win as they benefitted from a dropped catch to get over the line against RR, while after a winning start, LSG have dropped like a stone with four consecutive losses in their recent matches....Read More
At 9th and 10th in the table, the two teams have had campaigns to forget thus far. They are a far ways off from the playoff picture – and most would argue that they are already out of the running, with the quality of teams at the top of the table. But one thing the IPL continues to prove is that it's never too late while a mathematical possibility exists.
Nevertheless, a massive effort will be needed from the two teams to find the motivation and the consistency in performances. All of that will start as the teams get their campaigns started at the Ekana Stadium in Lucknow. KKR are coming in after a solid break, a week off after their first win, in which they team can take the momentum from a result and find real clarity in terms of what the team needs to achieve.
For LSG, the four match losing skid is certainly a massive concern. The players they have in their unit are not delivering to their potential, but there are still bright sparks – particularly in the bowling, where Mohammed Shami and Prince Yadav have been in top form, and ensured that they have a semblance of competitiveness.
This match provides a chance for LSG to join the teams on six points and at least be within swinging distance, while for KKR, getting a win is already non-negotiable.
LSG vs KKR Playing XIs
LSG XI: Mitchell Marsh, Aiden Markram, Rishabh Pant (c&wk), Ayush Badoni, Nicholas Pooran, Mukul Choudhary, George Linde, Digvesh Rathi, Mohammed Shami, Prince Yadav, Mohsin Khan
Impact: Himmat Singh, Akshat Raghuvanshi, Abdul Samad, Avesh Khan, Manimaran Siddharth
KKR XI: Ajinkya Rahane (c), Tim Seifert (wk), Angkrish Raghuvanshi, Cameron Green, Rovman Powell, Rinku Singh, Sunil Narine, Anukul Roy, Ramandeep Singh, Kartik Tyagi, Vaibhav Arora
Impact: Manish Pandey, Finn Allen, Tejasvi Singh, Navdeep Saini, Varun Chakravarthy

2 run chase. Should be all KKR.

For LSG, it's Pooran and Markram. Tyagi remembers a clutch over against them…

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: SLOT BALL AND MOHAMMED SHAMI TONKS IT TO KINGDOM COME, AS COOL AS YOU LIKE! Stands his ground and swing through it, clears long off – a dramatic last over, and Mohammed Shami sends it to IPL 2026's first super over!

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: Shami can't connect! Now it's simple – six to go through to a super over, otherwise KKR take the points. Shami rushed by the quicker one, that has to be the ploy again…

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: Mohammed Shami off sprinting with the ball, it's the right idea. Prince misses, but he makes it to the non-striker's end on time as well.
What can Shami do…

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: ANOTHER FLIP IN THE SCRIPT! Himmat tries to walk across and flip it over fine leg, but gets it too square! Rinku Singh is there and takes the catch!
Himmat Singh can't get it done! Great knock, 8 needed off 3 balls – what do LSG do?
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: HIMMAT COLLECTS FOUR! 8 NEEDED OFF 4
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: GREAT SHOT FROM HIMMAT SINGH! Expects the slower ball, waits for it, knows where he wants to hit it, and picks the gap!
Down to 8 off 4…
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: TWO BEAMERS IN AN OVER! Twist in the tale!
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: Another no-ball above the waist for Himmat Singh, who gets it deep enough this time to run two! Another no-ball given – it will be another free-hit!

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: NO-BALL! BUT NO MORE RUNS OFF IT

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: Shami swings through his shoes and can't connect, but Seifert can't collect! Over to Himmat Singh…

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: 38 needed off last 3 overs
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: Badoni gets away another boundary, but Kartik Tyagi stops the bleeding and ends the over well.
Tight finish coming up. Who would you rather be?
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: SIX FOR HIMMAT! LSG stay alive
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: Big hit to keep LSG in touching distance! Gets width and carves it for six over point.
45 needed off 22 – LSG still in it!
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: No Mukul showcase this time! Picks out Rinku in the deep
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: He did the unthinkable last time they met, and he needs something close to that again – but doesn't get it! Has to target the deep leg-side and doesn't get enough of it – comfortable catch for Rinku in the deep! KKR have their noses ahead.
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: GONEEEE!!! Pooran falls cheaply again
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: It simply doesn't get better for him! Nicholas Pooran gets a wide one from Chakravarthy, tries to cut it, but it's a thick edge that slices straight to third man inside the ring!

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: That one he gets a hold of! Wide of off-stump, but it's a safe shot with no fielder on the boundary. No fielder was stopping that anyway. Fetches him six – and important one too, will give him a lot of confidence.

LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: HUGE MOMENT! Rishabh Pant tries to pull ot the reverse sweep but can only get glove on it – sneaks through to Seifert, who makes the grab on his second attempt. Umpire initially unmoved, but it's a good review – brushes his glove and he's gone!
78/3 now – the pressure is on! Are KKR ahead now?
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: Required rate creeps above 10 – 82 needed from 48
LSG vs KKR LIVE Score: Getting slightly hairy. Pooran not connecting well at all. Pant bears so much of the responsibility over the remainder of this innings, with work to be done.

RR vs SRH Highlights, IPL 2026: Power against power – IPL 2026 is all set for what might be the most exciting match of the season thus far. It is a battle between Rajasthan Royals and Sunrisers Hyderabad at the Sawai Mansingh Stadium in Jaipur, and it is all about the power-packed batters on show. Four left-handed opening batters who absolutely love demolishing a cricket ball early on, three of this quartet Indian talent who are redefining the limited overs game. Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for the RR camp, and Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma across for them playing for RR....Read More
Although these six-hitting superstars headline this contest, there is much more riding on this contest. This match sees RR return to Jaipur after spending the first half of their campaign with Guwahati as their home venue – a return to Jaipur will also be assigned the responsibility of continuing their form and taking them to the playoffs, with the team in a good position at the moment.
But that could all change if SRH go on a run. A team with a dangerous batting unit – we mentioned that top two, but don’t forget Ishan Kishan soon after, and Heinrich Klaasen finding his power swing – which is also beginning to find the diamonds in the rough in the bowling department. Shivang Kumar and Sakib Hussain have been real gems, and what Praful Hinge did against the same opposition on debut is memorable.
But the big boost for SRH is the return of Pat Cummins – the Australian captain has been deemed fit to participate in the IPL over the second half of the campaign, and his joining the team suddenly makes it look very complete. There is some more batting depth, and there is an international superstar who they can turn to when the going gets tough.
SRH are currently on four wins and eight points, right behind RR on ten points with five wins. A win for SRH could be massive – their NRR is just a tick above RR’s, which means that a win for SRH tonight would see them vault into third place over the home team. It would also seal them well and truly in the running for the playoffs with the business end around the corner.
An exciting game with massive implications.
RR vs SRH Playing XIs
RR Playing XI: Yashasvi Jaiswal, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel (wk), Riyan Parag (c), Shimron Hetmyer, Donovan Ferreira, Ravindra Jadeja, Jofra Archer, Tushar Deshpande, Nandre Burger, Brijesh Sharma
Impact: Bishnoi, Pretorious, Sandeep, Dubey, Ravi Singh
SRH Playing XI: Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan, Heinrich Klaasen, Salil Arora (wk), Aniket Verma, Nitish Reddy, Pat Cummins (c), Shivang Kumar, Sakib Hussain, Eshan Malinga, Praful Hinge
Impact: Head, R Smaran, Livingstone, Dubey, Harshal

Done with time to spare – a day for big chases, a day for powerful batting to make their names bolder.

But Heinrich Klaasen! What a shot that is, very full from Deshpande, but it's excellent batting, waits on it and drives it over extra cover for a fantastic maximum.

Has forever to try and get underneath it but misjudges it – has to put a dive in at the last second, and ends up nowhere close to it! NKR survives.

Twist in the script here? SRH ahead of the game here, but wickets can have an influence.

RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Tell you waht, this has been nowhere close to his most brutal – a lot of patience and placement, picking the gaps and working the bowlers over. The shot to get to fifty is an example of it, low full toss wide of off stump, and he slices it away but with total control, all along the floor and easily away for four more.

RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Can you believe it – Jadeja puts down a sitter!
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Dropped catches are a part of cricket. But tell you what, you could put your house down on Ravindra Jadeja taking that simple catch right to him, and somehow you would lose it! No way he should be putting that one down, but it's straight to him and straight down. He will be so disappointed in himself.
Abhishek onto 30*(15).
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: 19 run over! SRH in the ascendancy
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: A little more emphatic now! It was a sketchy start but both these batters beginning to find their range. Abhishek gives himself room and finds four more off Burger – the South African has been expensive!
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Hetmyer loses it in the lights! Abhishek survives
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: What's happened there! Slashed hard towards deep third by Abhishek, but it looks to be right to Hetmyer – but the ball is lost in the lights, and he has no clue where it is! A terrific fielder, but simply doesn't pick it up and has to bow out from the catch.
Abhishek gets four – and punishes him next ball with six more!
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Kishan goes after Archer – 12 off the over
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Really entertaining battle here. Archer beats the bat a couple of times, but Kishan also has the measure of him to spear a couple of drives and punches through the off-side.
38/1 after 3 overs – chase is on!
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: 13 run over but RR finding purchase – edged over slip!
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Abhishek Sharma gets away with one! Goes hard at Nandre Burger, thick edge which just evades first slip. Some probing bowling from RR early on, they will want to remove one of the two Indian southpaws before they can cause damage.
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: NO MISTAKE THIS TIME! Jurel makes up, Archer still on fire
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Some ridiculous quality on show from Jofra Archer! Searing fast, leaving Head, produces another edge. Head is so tentative once again, an edge which carries for a simpler chance, and Jurel happy to take it this time!
Terrific early bowling from Archer, deserves this wicket.
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: TRAVIS HEAD DROPPED FIRST BALL! Archer is fuming
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Can you believe it! Fielding across both matches has been sloppy today. That was a tough chance, but you expect a glovesman of Jurel's quality to take it. Archer produces a genuine edge from Head, but it's always fading away from Jurel – both hands to it, but can't hold on! Archer is hopping mad.
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: RR finish on 228/6
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Hetmyer misses out on a juicy full-toss in the last over, falls on 11. In the end, RR reach 228/6, which is a solid score but within SRH's wheelhouse. Excellent performance at the death from them – 44 off the last 5 overs. Gives their batters a decent chance given their strength in the top order.
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Ferreira falls! Malinga strikes
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Big wicket with 9 balls to play! Hetmyer not quite hitting it well so this Ferreira scalp could be significant. Low full toss so Ferreira times it well but can't get the elevation on it. Flies to long-on, and well taken by the fielder there.
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: 6, 4, 6! Ferreira motoring
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Great hitting! Ensuring Sooryavanshi's base doesn't go to waste – Sakib is being met, proving expensive on this day in Jaipur. Overpitched and hammered, then too short and hammered again. Excellent hitting by Ferreira, very quick and compact swings to get the runs moving again after a couple of quiet overs.
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: 195/4 after 17; Cummins terrific
RR vs SRH LIVE Score: Excellent spell on return by Cummins – making Sunrisers look terrific. Ferreira on 16*(10), Hetmyer fresh to the crease, and will be looking at 230 from here. Three overs to play.

Royal Challengers Bengaluru vs Gujarat Titans Highlights: The Indian Premier League table is beginning to take shape, and it is that time of the season where it is a real muddle in the middle. Teams at the top dropping points, and teams nearer the bottom beginning to find results, meaning there are several candidates for who could make it through to the playoffs....Read More
A couple of weeks ago, Royal Challengers Bengaluru seemed to be one of the core of teams destined for the top four. Their powerful batting being stymied against Delhi Capitals has suddenly meant they can feel the breath of teams like SRH, DC, and even CSK down their necks – all of whom are flawed teams, but have the talent and the quality to make a winning stretch down the latter half of the season count.
This is the last match at the M Chinnaswamy for the league stage of the season – there is always a chance it will host the finals as the home of the defending champions, but as far as RCB’s home schedule goes, the last two matches will be played in Raipur. RCB started off with three consecutive wins at home, but Delhi Capitals cracked that shell thanks to a terrific bowling performance. Despite all their strengths, form has a role to play, and RCB look slightly fragile, something Gujarat’s excellent bowling attack might be interested in probing.
Gujarat themselves, of course, are in a spot of bother of their own. After three consecutive wins to bring them into the conversation for playoffs, a huge 99-run loss against Tilak Varma’s century and MI meant that their NRR took a tanking and all the good work was undone. It was a rare occasion when that terrific top three all fell early, and the remainder of the batting wasn’t able to come to the party.
A win for GT tonight would vault them over DC and CSK, onto eight points and right outside the playoff picture. A loss, on the other hand, would see them playing catch up with the teams ahead of them, reliant on results to help them out in the quest for fourth.
For RCB, a win takes them into second on 10 points, and will once again realign them with making a run for Top 2. A loss, however, would drag them into that little group beneath them, leave them there for the taking in the run-in for the season, there to be hunted down by the likes of SRH and CSK.
A match that has a big influence on how the remainder of their respective seasons look.
RCB vs GT Live Score: Krunal's cameo sees RCB home!

Back up into second place, and once again looking dominant. Back to winning ways, led by good bowling and Kohli's half-century, but a central thesis that suits this team so well.
That's all for tonight – RCB leave Bengaluru with momentum behind them, but question marks for GT to answer.

author: Kartikay Dutta
headline: Riyan Parag's confidence is RR's backbone, but he needs to stop being a passenger in a team hungry for the title
body:
Riyan Parag's confidence is RR's backbone, but he needs to stop being a passenger in a team hungry for the title
Riyan Parag has been a livewire in the field and with his tactics through IPL 2026, but all that will be for nothing if he still struggles for runs.
First, let’s start with the positives. Before even playing a match on their home ground of the Sawai Mansingh Stadium in Jaipur, Rajasthan Royals have five wins, ten points from seven games, and sit in second place in the table. Under Riyan Parag’s rookie leadership, the young and impressive Royals team seem to be on pace for the playoffs, where their power-hitting and well-rounded bowling will make them a threat.
Riyan Parag’s captaincy is a tick in the book – how he has utilised his bowling resources, been unafraid to top-end his overseas stars in Jofra Archer and Nandre Burger, and how well he has planned for some of the most destructive hitters in the league.
But for Parag on a personal level, there is a lot left to be desired. The captaincy hat is one which sits heavy on most heads, and Parag is one of the many in the IPL who have struggled to don it and keep it separate from his own performances on the field.
Parag failing to step up to the requirements
There have been some tactical missteps – giving himself an over against Rinku Singh and Anukul Roy, when Ravindra Jadeja still had an over to spare, and giving an ailing KKR a path to beat RR, is probably the most significant. But most crucial of all has been Parag’s failure to show up with the bat: 81 runs in 7 matches at the heart of RR’s order, a blank spot that is threatening to render all the good work moot.
Parag is surrounded by Indian talent that has made RR forces to be reckoned with. The untapped potential of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the steadily growing leadership of Yashasvi Jaiswal, the dependency of Dhruv Jurel. Following this top three, the Riyan Parag that was expected was a continuation of the player who hammered 950+ runs in the last two IPL seasons, with the bat speed and the gap-finding, to convert those starts into big scores for his team.
What was expected of Parag in that number four position is something akin to what Shreyas Iyer for PBKS and Rajat Patidar for RCB are providing – two players thriving under the captaincy, at the helm of two successful teams gunning for the title with some revolutionary batting in the middle of the order.
What's the solution for Parag?
But rather than being spurred on by the additional responsibility, Parag finds himself in the camp of players who were rewarded captaincy because of the quality, only to find themselves crushed by the pressure. Rishabh Pant, who continues to find it uneasy, or Ruturaj Gaikwad, who looks like a shadow of his former self since taking over from MS Dhoni.
Captaincy is a skill all of its own in cricket, and at least Parag is finding success there. But at the end of the day, you can’t choose your best captain in T20 cricket anymore, even in an impact player era where teams have additional resources: players like Parag have received the mantle because of how key they are to team spirit and to team performance. To lose their own contribution to that can prove fatal.
What’s going wrong for Parag is not an easy problem to diagnose – he is returning from some injury rehab and that might play a role, and Kumar Sangakkara believes that he is hitting it well and is not too far from producing a match-winning knock soon. Parag believes the same – it’s simply a question of walking the talk.
Is keeping batting separate from captaincy the right advice? It changes from player to player – Iyer, for example, is batting with the maturity of a leader, clearly made stronger by that additional responsibility. Patidar bats like he wants his team to bat, hell-for-leather, one of a long list of power hitters. There's no one-size-fits-all answer for what will work for Parag, but sooner rather than later, he needs to find what does.
Kartikay Dutta is a sports writer with the Hindustan Times Sports Desk, having joined in August 2024 to further his professional journey and pursue his long-standing ambition of working in sports media. With a strong interest in cricket, tennis, and football, Kartikay focuses on exploring the deeper layers of sport beyond the immediate action on the field. His work centres on producing insightful long-form and analytical pieces that examine the stories behind performances, teams, and tournaments. He is particularly interested in narratives that intersect with culture, history, and politics, helping readers understand how modern sport evolves within a broader social context. Before joining Hindustan Times, Kartikay worked with leading digital newsrooms in India, where he developed a strong foundation in data-driven storytelling. He is skilled at using statistics, records, and historical trends to uncover patterns and perspectives that add depth to match coverage and sports features. His approach combines research with narrative clarity, allowing complex sporting developments to be presented in an engaging and accessible manner. Kartikay also has a growing passion for multimedia storytelling. He actively explores how photographs, videos, and graphics can enhance sports journalism by illustrating not only what happens during competition but also the preparation, strategy, and operational dynamics behind the scenes. Through this evolving skill set, he aims to contribute to a more immersive and modern form of sports storytelling that connects audiences more closely with the games they follow.Read More

Royal Challengers Bengaluru vs Delhi Capitals, IPL 2026 LIVE Score: At the moment, the Indian Premier League is beginning to look like a very stratified, disjointed table – there are teams struggling to piece together any amounts of good cricket, teams which are overperforming, teams which have quality but are flawed, and then a couple of teams who look absolutely unbeatable, especially if the conditions are in their favour....Read More
Royal Challengers Bengaluru are defending champions and are playing like it – five matches, four wins, and three out of three on their home patch at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium. In a near-perfect season last year, their home form was the one small concern, and the thing they needed to work on entering the fresh season. They have done just that – with five matches in Bengaluru, they have already racked up three wins.
There is a chance to make it four, but to do so, they will need to come good against a team that beat them at this venue last year, Delhi Capitals. With the talismanic KL Rahul playing a near-perfect chase from the middle order in that match, he returns to his hometown, this time on his birthday – some would say the stars are aligning for Rahul to kickstart the middle portion of DC's season. After two wins early, they lost two games they would have fancied themselves in, losing chases from comfortable positions.
In RCB, they have a team which looks completely incapable of losing a game when batting second. The best hope for teams against RCB is usually to restrict them and then play the matchups to hunt them down, but this is also a team capable of batting first and hammering 240, as they did against MI, or 250, as they did against CSK. There is hardly a weakness in this team, and they will start every match as favourites.
DC have the talent, and the bowling ability and batting quality to stay competitive in every match – but at 2-2, that's not good enough, and will need that extra oomph. With plenty on the line, should make for a thriller.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: HE'S DONE IT! Miller gets DC over the line
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: A small hiccup, but nothing more! David Miller rises to the occasion at the Chinnaswamy, and proves that the belief he had in himself a couple of weeks ago was not unfounded! It's full, Miller just needs to pierce the infield, and does so with ease!
DC win with a ball to spare – ups and downs in their chase once again, but the points on the board are just that! Up into fourth for them.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: MILLER GETS SIX MORE! Just 1 needed off 2!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Misses his length again and travels out of the stadium again! Too much width and Miller pumps it far, far over extra cover! Beautiful from Miller – this should have settled it!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: BANG! Killer Miller sends it deep for six
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: BOOM! Don't miss your length! A full toss to David Miller, he has made a career hitting those for six! Keeps flying!
7 needed off 3…
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: 1 again! 13 needed off 4 balls
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Stubbs can't get it away either! Not big enough from Stubbs, times it well but not in the gap! It was there to hit too!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Romario last over – 1 off the first!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Romario Shepherd's first over of the night, to Miller… MISSES OUT! On his legs, and he simply whiffs! Can only come through for the single!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: 15 needed off the last over!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Only 10 off Rasikh's over! Good lengths – concedes one four but gives the last over 15 to defend.
Can Miller get it over the line this time?
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: 25 needed off last 2 overs
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Getting very close here. DC need to get some boundaries off in the next over – RCB have bowled out Hazlewood and Bhuvneshwar, and need Rasikh then maybe Romario or Suyash to step up.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Stubbs finally connects for a big six!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: He has struggled with his scoring rate today but that's a really important shot! Hadn't been a boundary in 24 balls, this one is in short from Bhuvi – Stubbs has to hop, but gets so much power behind it to send it for six.
28 needed off 14.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Excellent Hazlewood over; 37 needed off 3
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Things are getting sketchy for DC! Just 22 runs off the last 24 balls – they have played themselves into a tough finish. Not great from the chasing team.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Axar goes off injured! Cramps – things tougher
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Axar Patel down with cramps yet again – he's not doing too well physically. Hobbles off with two physios.
Stubbs and Miller now. Need 41 off 24.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: 59 needed off 42 – Axar counter-attacked!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Great response from Axar Patel. Quiet tournament for him with the bat this far but playing excellent shots on the off-side to get boundaries. Rate well within control.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: KL Rahul gone! Kohli with a great catch, huge moment
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: WHAT A TAKE THAT IS! KL can't quite pick the gap, and Kohli is on hand to shuttle across and take a really good catch!
DC not out of the woods yet – over to the middle order once againu.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: 50/3 after powerplay
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Good rebuild from DC after things looked very shaky. A long way to go in this one still.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Rahul looks in supreme touch! Torches Hazlewood
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Terrific from Rahul! Making sure he doesn't let the rate get out of hand, wants to keep DC ahead of the match despite the early wickets. Three fours in that over.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: ANOTHER SENSATIONAL CATCH! Jitesh sends Rizvi packing
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: RCB's fielding making a real impact! Genuine edge from Rizvi flies to Jitesh's right, and his only hope is to leap across and stick out a paw. It's low to the ground, and he makes it stick!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Karun gone! Stellar catch from Salt
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: What a grab! Salt at deep point has to cover a lot of ground to his left, but he's rapido – gets across and puts in a great dive to hold on to it!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Hazlewood taken down by KL Rahul!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Excellent batting from the hometown birthday boy! Knows this is the main bowler and knows he has to take responsibility. Hammers a six and a four. Great start.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Chase begins – AND NISSANKA IS GONE!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Early wicket! Bhuvneshwar Kumar who does the trick! Seams in and Nissanka is late to get his bat down. He's a fair way across his stumps, and although the umpire gives it not out, review gets RCB a crucial breakthrough!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: RCB set target of 176
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Excellent, excellent bowling at the death from Delhi to restrict RCB. They were set for close to 210-220 with ease, but wickets through the second half on a grippy surface has set up a chase where DC are the favourites.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Run out now! Krunal sold down the river
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Miscommunication! Last over and Jitesh wants the strike so rushes down the other end, but Krunal not interested. Rahul can lob it to Ngidi, who whips off the bails for the run-out.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: PLUMB! Kuldeep removes Shepherd
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Key wicket again! Romario can cause so much damage at the depth, but Kuldeep forces a mistake. It's too full to go for that sweep, sneaks under his bat and hits hit adjacent. That was always out – a desperate review doesn't help.
149/6 now. Excellent bowling.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: ANOTHER WICKET! Axar gets Tim David
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: GREAT BOWLING! Axar Patel floats it up outside off-stump, David tries to go hard. Just a touch of grip off the surface, and it loops comfortably to short third. Easily taken, 5 down now.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Mukesh provides a free-hit, but no harm
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Mukesh Kumar goes short too often and it's a no-ball for deliveries above the shoulder. Jitesh on strike trying to take it on, but a big top edge goes nowhere.
146/4 after 15 overs.
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: GOT HIM! Mukesh removes Patidar
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: HUGE MOMENT! RCB were counter attacking with David and Patidar going hard. Mukesh goes wide of the crease after conceding six, and coaxes an edge from the RCB skipper. Well taken by Rahul, and there's suddenly something for the hosts to worry about!
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: SALT GOES! Kuldeep has him dismissed
RCB vs DC LIVE Score: Salt departs! Can't kick on. Kuldeep Yadav has his man. Not enough distance on this attacking shot – Stubbs in the deep, and take it easily.
105/3 now.

author: Kartikay Dutta
headline: This is the year for Punjab Kings: Shreyas Iyer's team showing same hunger to end drought as RCB in IPL 2025
body:
This is the year for Punjab Kings: Shreyas Iyer's team showing same hunger to end drought as RCB in IPL 2025
Punjab Kings sit at the top of the tree with nine points early on in the season – there is something different about them this year. What is working for them?
Is this the year for Punjab Kings? That is the question on the lips of fans of the IPL, as PBKS stretched their unbeaten run to five matches and regained the top of the table in the 2026 Indian Premier League. For the franchise with no trophies to their name, every year begins with the question of whether the team can go the distance. For the first time in many years, it feels like the answer is yes.
Punjab did reach the final in 2025, but for a young team in the first year of a fresh cycle, one they entered with a single retention in vice-captain Shashank Singh, it was a question of testing the waters and seeing how far they could actually go. It was a breath of fresh air as Shreyas Iyer and Ricky Ponting joined forces – a successful duo in Delhi.
Last year, it was more aspirational than anything else. Punjab needed a reset, and they got one as they racked up 19 points and finished top of the table. It was the energy of a young upstart team, led by a domestic core and following the modern T20 tactics of batting as deep as possible and going as hard as possible.
What gives Punjab in 2026 their X-factor
Things are different in 2026. Now a known quantity, they have gone from being punchy underdogs to being genuine threats. The quality in their team makes it look like a stacked unit, but it’s the fashion in which they are racking up wins that truly makes them look like they are a team that believes they are meant for the championship.
Already this season, they have chased down 205, 220, and now 196 with consummate ease – with a combined 36 deliveries to spare across them. They have hardly broken a sweat and find themselves in first place and in pole position to make the playoffs yet again, only having ended a ten-year drought from the final four last time out.
For a team like Punjab, as it was for Royal Challengers Bengaluru in 2025, the energy and the mentality is different when it comes to a question of ending a long barren spell. What PBKS feel like in 2026 is not too different to what RCB felt like in 2025 (and indeed continue to feel like in 2026) – a winning machine, one that knows exactly what it needs to do in every single game, and does it with a Terminator-like precision.
What is working for Punjab starts at the very top – maybe the best tactician in the league in Ponting, with the charisma of Iyer at the head of a young team that has domestic stars like Arshdeep Singh, Priyansh Arya, and Prabhsimran Singh, all of whom who have proven to be some of the most efficient cricketers in their roles in the tournament. Supplement that with the experience of Stoinis and Chahal, it is a recipe for success – and one that is succeeding.
The power of Punjab's momentum
In the modern-day IPL, things are so level in terms of quality, scouting, and analytics that it often becomes a question of momentum. The resources and a good idea of how to use them are in place, but what the inertia of good performances can do is unlike anything else. RCB entered 2025 after a rollercoaster end to 2024 where they made the playoffs after being in last place in the table and racing away to six consecutive wins to end the season.
Of course, things don’t always translate – Mumbai Indians had a similar late-season remontada to reach the playoffs last year, but have looked completely off-colour to start this season with poor performances. But Punjab haven’t faced that downslide: they needed to start the season well when they had a relatively easy run of fixtures, and have just done that with truly comprehensive performances.
Now is when the tricky part of the season begins for PBKS – they have a match against LSG on Sunday, but then a near-week break before they face strong Delhi, Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Sunrisers in an 11-day period. Their key will be to ensure they don’t lose pace in that six-day gap, and that they can find the results even when situations aren’t going in their favour.
Kartikay Dutta is a sports writer with the Hindustan Times Sports Desk, having joined in August 2024 to further his professional journey and pursue his long-standing ambition of working in sports media. With a strong interest in cricket, tennis, and football, Kartikay focuses on exploring the deeper layers of sport beyond the immediate action on the field. His work centres on producing insightful long-form and analytical pieces that examine the stories behind performances, teams, and tournaments. He is particularly interested in narratives that intersect with culture, history, and politics, helping readers understand how modern sport evolves within a broader social context. Before joining Hindustan Times, Kartikay worked with leading digital newsrooms in India, where he developed a strong foundation in data-driven storytelling. He is skilled at using statistics, records, and historical trends to uncover patterns and perspectives that add depth to match coverage and sports features. His approach combines research with narrative clarity, allowing complex sporting developments to be presented in an engaging and accessible manner. Kartikay also has a growing passion for multimedia storytelling. He actively explores how photographs, videos, and graphics can enhance sports journalism by illustrating not only what happens during competition but also the preparation, strategy, and operational dynamics behind the scenes. Through this evolving skill set, he aims to contribute to a more immersive and modern form of sports storytelling that connects audiences more closely with the games they follow.Read More

Lucknow Super Giants vs Gujarat Titans Highlights, IPL 2026: The first portion of the IPL is almost through, as each team have had enough time to diagnose their own strength and weaknesses. It's still to early to say where each team could end up – there is plenty of time left in the tournament – but there are already camps being formed within the league table....Read More
Lucknow Super Giants and Gujarat Titans are two teams who are currently playing catch-up, not quite at the top of the table but enough strengths to have the upside to make it. For both, their most recent match demonstrated how their weaknesses can be detrimental, but how their strengths often come through to bring them crucial results.
Mukul Choudhary announced himself to the tournament with a splendid half-century to pull off a miracle chase for LSG, and will give confidence and add beef to question marks over the health of that team's middle order. If LSG's middle order can translate, they suddenly look like a very dangerous, high-quality team.
GT's top order came to the party, as they are expected to, and their bowling remains completely well-rounded. The big boost for them is how Washington Sundar has found some form in the middle order, but even more significant is how Rashid Khan seems to have found some of his own self.
If Rashid Khan can keep bowling like he did against DC, he beefs up the GT bowling unit in a way which means even the relative lack of explosion with their middle order isn't a problem: in their top three, they have a near-guarantee of runs.
The two teams are evenly-matched and close to each other in the table – LSG have the chance to enter the top three of the table, while GT have the chance to match them, DC, and RCB on four points.

Only one result from here.

LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Another fifty for Shubman Gill. No risk, easy in 34 balls. Hasn't had to exert much.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: GT need 71 off 54

LSG vs GT LIVE Score: All a little too easy for Gill. Short and wide from Linde, Gill needs to place it, and he does so twice with ease.
101 needed off 78.

LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Digvesh Rathi comes on and it's a loosener down the leg-side. Sudharsan tries to flick it over fine leg inside the circle but doesn't get enough of it – bad ball to get a wicket with but Rathi doesn't mind, his signature signature celebration.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Gill gets a move on! 4, 6, 4
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Good hitting from Gill! Shami drifts and he flicks for four, then on his stumps and it's a lovely straight drive that hits the boundary cushion on the full.
Next ball, timing to poke it through the off-side and evades all the fielders to pick up four more.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: GILL DROPPED! Hard chance for Prince
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Half-chance! Fired back at the bowler from Gill, and Prince Yadav gets his left hand up but it's too quick – jams his thumb and he needs attendance. Shami already injured and off the field.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: 17/0 after 2
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: A handful of boundaries early on, but also a couple of uppish shots – there is a little bit of something in this pitch.

LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Here we go then. 8 rpo – Gill, Sudharsan, Buttler will like their chances.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: LSG finish on 164/8!
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Bonus runs and LSG will be happy with those – some really crucial boundaries after Mukul's wicket, and 165 is something to bowl at, even if you fancy the chasers. Linde, Avesh, Shami all picking up runs.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Some important boundaries for GT – Shami gets six!
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Runs worth their weight in gold right now! Three fours off Prasidh's over just to mess his figures up a little bit.
Ashok Sharma on for the 20th, and is greeted with a big six! What timing from Shami, clean hit flies all the way for six!
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Not today for Mukul! Prasidh bouncer undoes him
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Four for Prasidh Krishna and LSG are about to limp to a finish. Hard to hit that anywhere – the bounce works again. Thin edge, easily taken by Buttler.
4/13 now, Krishna.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Mukul gets six off Rabada!
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: There he goes – wants to try and repeat those heroics to push this score towards 170, and starts with six off Rabada.
The South African very off-colour today.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Samad can't accelerate! Ashok gets him
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Innings continues to derail. Samad tries to go hard but can only splice it to midwicket.
118.6 with 3.4 overs to play. George Linde gets his debut.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: GONE! Prasidh with his third, Pooran departs
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Wickets keep coming for Prasidh! Big bouncer, up above Pooran's head, and it's a bad shot – tries to swat it down the ground and carves it straight to Gill.
Bad shot selection – well wide, that shot was never getting him runs. 109/5 now, brings in Mukul.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Pooran just manages to clear the boundary off Rashid!
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Rashid Khan nearly has Pooran again! Tempts Pooran into the hit and it goes very very very high – but also crucially just about long enough! Evades the fielder.
The next shot is a lot more convincing – inside out and much deeper, 12 off the last two balls.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: 83/4 after 11, no boundaries in nearly 5 overs
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: LSG struggling. Runs not coming. Pooran can accelerate but trying to play himself in, and as is Abdul Samad.
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Another for Prasidh! Badoni picks out square leg
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Prasidh continues to be amongst the wickets! Dug into Badoni's body, who tries to swing away – but hits it straight to square leg, where Phillips just about picks it out against the low sun!
74/4 out of nowhere – cracks showing!
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: 4, 4, out! One risk too many from Markram
LSG vs GT LIVE Score: Markram flirting with danger throughout that over – first hits it in Phillips' vicinity, who grabs it but can't stop it from running for four, then gets it half a foot above outstretched Sudharsan for four more. The next ball, tries to take on midwicket, but is rushed by Prasidh – can't clear deep midwicket!
Advantage GT now – 69/3.

PBKS vs SRH Highlights, IPL 2026: Punjab Kings are once again looking amongst the favourites to make a deep run in the IPL, even if they might think they lost a golden chance to extend a winning run against the ailing KKR as the match was rained out in Eden Gardens earlier this week. Nevertheless, five points and still unbeaten, and still in a terrific position early in the season – Shreyas Iyer's team are still playing with a chip on their shoulder, and as Ricky Ponting has proven again and again, that is when he is usually at his best....Read More
Back in New Chandigarh for their second of four home games at this venue, a sweltering day game gives them an opportunity to grab a win and keep pace with runaway leaders Rajasthan Royals early on. But in their way are Sunrisers Hyderabad – a team which hasn't quite clicked with its dangerous batting, but knows it has the potential to be the scariest team in the tournament if it does.
SRH are struggling with the fact that even a spirited set of performances from their depleted and weakened bowling unit hasn't been enough to collect more than two points from their three games thus far. The bowling doesn't have a go-to name and is patchy, but there are enough skilled players to defend targets or to restrict opposition – but for that to have an effect, they need the batting to click into gear.
While the likes of Travis Head, Abhishek Sharma, and Ishan Kishan are still waiting to leave their mark on the tournament, the bright side has been the form of Nitish Kumar Reddy, who is once again looking like a premier all-rounder after a quiet year last year. Along with him, Heinrich Klaasen has slowly been finding his touch, but is seeking that x-factor which makes him a six-hitting machine. Aniket Verma brings up the back end of this middle order unit, and adds power, youth, and exuberance aplenty.
The issue is that they are running into a team which has arguably the best sense of identity in the entire tournament. Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings have an Indian batting core with license to shoot for the starts, and a bowling unit that has every base covered. They know how they want to play cricket, and it works to give them points on points. PBKS know this is a ripe opportunity to separate themselves from names like Delhi Capitals, Lucknow, and the remainder of the teams who are sitting in that 4-7 range of the table – establish themselves as the team to beat, and counterpunch their way to success from there.
PBKS vs SRH Playing XIs
SRH: Sunrisers Hyderabad: Abhishek Sharma, Travis Head, Ishan Kishan (c & wk), Heinrich Klaasen, Salil Arora, Aniket Verma, Nitish Kumar Reddy, Harsh Dubey, Shivang Kumar, Harshal Patel, Eshan Malinga
PBKS: Prabhsimran Singh (wk), Priyansh Arya, Cooper Connolly, Shreyas Iyer (c), Shashank Singh, Marcus Stoinis, Marco Jansen, Xavier Bartlett, Vijaykumar Vyshak, Arshdeep Singh, Yuzvendra Chahal
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Game over! Punjab remain unbeaten
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: There's the winning runs from Shashank Singh! Punjab are beginning to look like a winning machine. This becomes their 10th chase of 200+ in franchise history, and Shreyas Iyer is making a habit of making them look easy.
They consolidate their position at second in the table. SRH, meanwhile, back to the drawing board with questions to answer.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: One hit away!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Shashank Singh hammers a big hit over the off-side and it's only one hit away now with 2 overs to go! Comprehensive from PBKS.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Timber! Wadhera dismissed
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Maybe too little too late – yorker snuck in by Harsh Dubey, and Nehal can't jam it out.
18 needed off 18.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Fifty for Shreyas Iyer as he launches into SRH!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Simply brutal! Two sixes and a four off the over and PBKS are running away with it now!
Shreyas means business. He is fighting for an Indian spot. 23 needed off 24.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Six more! Just 41 needed off 30 now
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Shreyas Iyer motoring! Took a couple of quiet overs but has had enough now, picks up another six. Wants to settle this early.
Onto 43*(22).
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Six more for Shreyas!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Hit-me ball! Bouncer dug in as a cutter into the surface, and Shreyas has all the time in the world to pick his spot and pick six.
Next ball, drives down the ground, something of a misfield and it's another four.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Punjab need 59 off 36
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: They will likely see it home from here. SRH don't have enough quality to throw at them late – Harshal Patel needs to find form of old.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: 6, 4! Iyer settling nerves
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Just what was needed for PBKS! 10 runs off 2 balls and Punjab continue to have their noses ahead. Picks the slower one and tonks it for an easy six, then prepared for the length to be pulled so cuts it very nicely for four more.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Shivang Kumar gets his third! Removes Connolly
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Another potentially big moment! Shivang Kumar ensuring SRH slowly pulling things back – he takes his third wicket, tossed up wide of off, Connolly tries to hammer it but can only slice it right up!
PBKS still in control, but this is becoming a little interesting.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Fifty and out! Prabhsimran gone, Shivang leads comeback
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: HUGE moment! Prabhsimran gets to his fifty with a four, but then he accounts for the second PBKS opener! Tossed up outside off and gets enough turn to generate the mishit as Prabh tries to go down the ground – sliced, and very nicely taken by Klaasen at long-off!
The game is on but SRH need to keep chipping away.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Priyansh finally dismissed! It's 99/1
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Shivang Kumar concedes six but gets a wicket the next ball! Priyansh doesn't want to slow down after the conclusion of the powerplay, but the field is spread and this time he can't find the stands with the big hit.
99/1 now – can SRH fight back?
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: 16-ball half-century for Priyansh!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Unreal. We have already seen 197 runs in the 11.4 powerplay overs thus far today.
6, 6, 4, 4 goes Arya. Runs shouldn't be this easy to score!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: 6, 6, dropped! Prabhsimran in top for and gets a bonus
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Dropped off Unadkat's own bowling! Chipped to him at a good height, and that's a chance you have to take after a batter has taken you for two sixes!
Prabhsimran racing away. Fifty partnership up. Making easy work of it thus far – 55/0 off 4.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: 4, 6! What Travishek can do…
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Prabhsimran and Priyansh can do too! Prabhsimran Singh takes a liking to Unadkat – dances down and hits it the distance and then some.
It's 29/0 after 2 overs. Pressure on early for SRH.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: 18 off the first over!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Very questionable tactics from SRH. Left arm spin to someone in as good form as Priyansh at the moment – gets the punishment as expected. Two fours and a six, easy 18 off the over. Chase is on.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Off with the chase – first ball four!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: SRH tempting Priyansh Arya early with the left-arm spin of Harsh Dubey and that's the risk – sweeps hard and safe for four off the first ball.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: SRH finish on 219/6
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: 105/0 in the first six overs, 114/6 in the next 14. One of the best starts in IPL history, but at the end of it, the momentum is with Punjab Kings.
220 is not impossible to chase at all. Punjab are very much in this.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Klaasen can't connect! His struggle ends
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Not his day! The big South African simply couldn't get going. 39 off 33 – not what you expect from him. He knows that he hasn't capitalised on the start that his team gave him.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Arshdeep gets a second! Sends Salil back
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Arshdeep has leaked runs but he has gotten a couple of wickets too. It's somewhat there to be hit, but angling away from Salil – tough to hit that down the ground, sliced up and easily taken.
214/5, 7 balls left.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Klaasen finally connects for six!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: He has struggled today to find his rhythm but that could be what triggers a big finish! Overpitched on his legs and it's a nice easy swing to find six.
206/4 with two to play.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: 6, 4, run-out! Aniket's charge prematurely ended
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Big wicket for Punjab! Aniket was getting into his groove with a couple of big hits off Chahal, but miscommunication between him and Klaasen sees him run out!
195/4 now with 20 balls to play.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: GONE! Jansen plucks one-handed catch out of nowhere
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Arshdeep has some joy and he has the big mitts of his fast bowling partner to thank! Kishan flicks it well but not far enough from Jansen – he takes some special catches and tell you what, there was no reason for this one to be that good! He was beaten by the pace of it a bit so didn't opt to go with two hands – just the one massive hand stuck out, and very easily taken!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Kishan fetches six off Arshdeep!
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: First six in a while, and it is Arshdeep who continues to get the punishment! Not his day at all. Goes for the searing yorker but just misses full, and Kishan whips it for six.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: 143/2 after 11
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Run rate has dropped significantly and it is still at 13. Klaasen and Kishan getting set – Punjab turn to Jansen because of the danger that presents.
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: ONE MORE FOR SHASHANK! Gets Abhishek as well
PBKS vs SRH LIVE Score: Can you imagine it! Shashank Singh has turned out to be the golden-est of arms! Wide of off to Abhishek, gets a good piece of it, but not good enough! He hits it straight to Arshdeep who takes a good catch on the boundary!
PBKS clawing ground back.

headline: RR vs RCB Highlights, IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel's whirlwind stand keeps RR's unbeaten run intact
body:
RR vs RCB Highlights, IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel's whirlwind stand keeps RR's unbeaten run intact
Rajasthan Royals vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru Highlights, IPL 2026: RR sink RCB and send their message loud and clear to the remainder of the IPL! Four wins, four games, and it is Vaibhav Sooryavanshi who leads the charge with an incredible 78(26). RCB simply blown away in a battle for the top.

RR vs RCB Highlights, IPL 2026: Nearly two weeks into the IPL, and things are beginning to heat up. A pair of real thrillers have greeted our screens over the last two days, bringing the tournament to life with a pair of immaculate last-ball finishes. Those were responsible for shaking things up in the bottom half of the table – but today's focus, as we return to Guwahati, is at the very top, where the victor of today's game is expected to take first place....Read More
Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru have thumped their way to two comfortable wins – the opening encounter against SRH, making easy work of a 200+ chase, before demolishing CSK thanks to Tim David's onslaught. With all bases covered and going from strength to strength, RCB are confident, and a winning machine – at the moment, they look like a lock for the playoffs. Who can really stop them?
How about a teenager who faced Jasprit Bumrah and hit the first ball for six? If going from strength to strength is the thing, then how can you look beyond Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The 15-year-old has started IPL 2026 with a bang, and has been a big part of the reason why Rajasthan Royals are sitting in first place with three wins from three matches. His destructive starts have been given able support by Yashasvi Jaiswal, who has a chip on his shoulder and something to prove himself – but it is the bowling group which has been the most impressive, making RR look like playoff candidates.
This is a true test for RR to prove that accreditation as potential runners in the IPL's trophy chase. A win in Guwahati tonight would help them leave their second home with four wins from four games, on eight points and heads and shoulders ahead of the rest of the league. But an impressive performance is necessary – RCB are gifted with so many different ways of hurting the opposition, and despite RR's fiery nature, probably start this match as favourites.
RR vs RCB Likely Playing XIs:
RR XII: Yashasvi Jaiswal, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel (wk), Riyan Parag (c), Shimron Hetmyer, Ravindra Jadeja, Donovan Ferreira, Jofra Archer, Nandre Burger, Ravi Bishnoi, Sandeep Sharma, Tushar Deshpane
RCB XII: Virat Kohli, Phil Salt, Devdutt Padikkal, Rajat Patidar (c), Tim David, Jitesh Sharma (wk), Romario Shepherd, Krunal Pandya, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Suyash Sharma, Jacob Duffy, Abhinandan Singh
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: That's it! RR make it 4 from 4
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Game over! Took 18 overs to do it, but RR wanted the points first and foremost – Jurel was at the scene so many times as they struggled to take it home last year, but there's no doubt as he takes it home with 81* tonight.
RR are the pacesetters. RR have the Orange Cap and Purple Cap. RR are the team to beat.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Things dawdling towards an end
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: The end arriving with a whisper rather than a bang. 9 needed off 16. RR not showing much urgency – don't have to.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Just 25 needed off 30 – has Jurel iced it?
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: A lovely square drive then a fortunate outside edge for a pair of four s-- Jurel onto 61* and that may settle it as far as the certainty of the result is concerned.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: FIFTY for Jurel!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Off just 25 balls, and that is despite a slow-down in recent overs just to calm things down.
His second fifty of the tournament – enjoying life at number 3 for the Royals.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Jurel and Jadeja calm things down, 48 off 42 required
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Two players who have been involved in Test cricket for India, so you don't mind them being the ones to shepherd the rest of this chase. Will need to be careful in the three overs of Krunal, Hazlewood, and Bhuvneshwar left – but RCB committing their resources early.
48 needed off 42 after Pandya bowls out.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Now Parag falls! Hara-kiri from RR
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: No need! Yes, constant attack is the name of the game in modern T20s, but sometimes reading the situation still has value. Play out Josh Hazlewood here and RR are probably home safe – but Parag decides to attack and loses his wicket by picking out Kohli at long-on!
RR suddenly back in this.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Two in two! Krunal gets Hetmyer with a bouncer
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Shimron Hetmyer appears without a helmet so Krunal challenges him with a bouncer – and the big Guyanese will want that back! Guides it straight to short third, catching practice. Suddenly RCB fighting back a little.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: End of the night for Sooryavanshi! 78(26) and no typos there
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Doesn't quite get a hold of this one and Virat Kohli takes the catch. But he has brought this match into RR's territory, already down to run-a-ball.
What an innings. Krunal strikes but take nothing away.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: No stopping RR now
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: This is unbelievable. Sooryavanshi takes on Krunal for six – but even Jurel enjoying himself. Finding the gaps and picking fours for fun.
Some incredible batting. RR are in monstrous form.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Jurel joins the party – 97/1 off the powerplay! Wow
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: These are the defending champions, but this pair are making them look amateurish. The bowling has been mostly alright too – but Jurel casually contributes a 24-run over himself!
It's 97/1 after 6 overs. Wow.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: SOORYAVANSHI WITH ANOTHER 15-BALL FIFTY! On a different level
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Absolute joke! In his last six balls, he goes 4, 4, 4, 6, 6, 6 – and it's another 15-ball half-century for him, matching his own record!
Mind you, those six balls against Hazlewood and Bhuvi. Two of the greatest of their generation. What on earth!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Sooryavanshi gets 4 on his first ball off Hazlewood and then 4 more!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: He doesn't care! It's maybe too close to cut Hazlewood again, but even cramped, he manages to get enough power to help it run away for four. Next ball pitched higher, and Sooryavanshi flips him over mid-on for four more!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: First Sooryavanshi six with absolute ease! 16 off the over
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Terrific! On his pads and overpitched, and it's so easy for him there. A simple putaway for four, but to get enough strength to find the six there – this is one special talent, no way it should look that easy.
37/1 after 3.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: 6, 6, out! Hazlewood gets last laugh over Jaiswal
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: The Jaiswal-Hazlewood battle is an interesting one – in roughly 6 overs' worth of bowling, Jaiswal has now pilfered over 90 runs, but also been dismissed 4 times!
Carved over point, then hooked over square, but the last ball of the over is too close to him to cut – edge flies through to the keeper and Hazlewood has his first of the tournament!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Back for the big chase!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Should be a really intriguing chase – but Jaiswal and Sooryavanshi will want to settle it early. Bhuvneshwar Kumar first up with ball.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Big six and RCB finish on 201/8!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Brought in for some late batting and Venkatesh Iyer delivers! Overpitched outside the off-stump and he doesn't try to over-hit, goes with it and finds six more.
This will be a real chase now. The two MP boys helping out big time.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: 4, 6! Venky connects
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Big hit! First the reverse for four, then in his slot and he can deposit it over midwicket for six!
195 with last ball coming up…
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: 180/8 with last over coming up
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Last over with Venky Iyer on strike – can he get a few big hits off Sandeep?
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Sandeep ends Patidar's terrific innings!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Sandeep Sharma effective as ever late in the innings! A wobbler which is in the slot, but it's so tough to generate power off him – Patidar has been hitting it so well, but he can only pick out deep midwicket.
166/8 – Iyer needs to dominate strike now.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: 6, 6! Patidar demolishes Bishnoi
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Getting into his work now! He loves batting late in innings, and he loves hitting spin – even someone as good as Bishnoi getting the treatment! Misses his length twice, and Patidar finds the crowd twice. Huge hitting!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Fifty for Patidar! 150/7 with 4 to play
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: A true captain's knock, keeping things alive even as everything goes wrong around him. A supremely well-timed cover drive for four – he has looked really good. Wants to make a case to play for India, and doing himself no harm at all.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: 6, 6! Patidar begins to motor
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: RCB bring in the extra batter, Venkatesh Iyer earning his RCB debut. But it is captain Patidar who gets moving against Nandra Burger – a lovely off-drive for six, then a pickup that flies miles back into the crowd!
139/7 with 5 overs to play – while Patidar is there, RCB could get anywhere.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: SHEPHERD GONE! Jadeja strikes
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Just no partnerships! Shepherd too early through a big swipe on leg stump, he will feel that should be in the stands – but the top edge goes easily to Brijesh at short fine leg. He's a little casual so the ball pops out first time of trying, but he is lucky to snap it up second time around. Can breathe easy!
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Shepherd up and running – RCB bat long
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: This is a big part of why RCB were champions – a player as devastating as Shepherd in at 8. Gets the first six of the innings, then a pair of fours off Brijesh.
119/6. Runs were drying up, so important to re-inject some momentum.
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Things quiet – and the pressure tells! Hetmyer with another catch, Tim David sent back
RR vs RCB LIVE Score: Another big moment! The innings was meandering bit and that isn't how RCB like to play – Tim David wanted to get a move on so tries to hammer Brijesh, but doesn't get it far enough wide of Hetmyer. The West Indian is well-positioned, and it's a good catch at a comfortable height.
94/6 – RCB in trouble!
