author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Vaibhav Suryavanshi: “Mere papa ne kaam chhor diya, meri maa...”: Cricketer Vaibhav Suryavanshi reveals why he will always remain indebted to his parents - The Times of India
body: Vaibhav Suryavanshi’s blistering 50 off just 15 balls may have lit up the scoreboard, but the real impact of the moment came after the innings, when he spoke about the people standing quietly behind the scenes of his rise. In a sport that often celebrates power, timing and fearless intent, his words brought the spotlight back to something far more enduring: sacrifice. The young batter’s explosive knock was memorable on its own. But when he opened up about his family’s struggle, the innings took on a deeper meaning. It was no longer just about sixes and strike rates. It was about a mother sleeping for only a few hours so her son could train. It was about a father giving up work. It was about an elder brother taking over responsibilities at home. And it was about a household that kept moving forward, even when life felt heavy. Scroll down to read more... 5 Feb 2026 | 23:15 Let’s Talk Parenting: What Has It Taught You So Far? The innings that turned heads Vaibhav’s 15-ball half-century was the kind of knock that stops a crowd mid-breath. Fast, fearless and clean, it showed a batter playing with rare confidence and no visible hesitation. Every shot seemed to carry the same message: he belonged on that stage. There was a rhythm to his innings that went beyond technique, a calm control that made even the most aggressive shots feel measured rather than reckless, as if he was playing both the moment and the expectations around him. There was a quiet assurance in the way he picked his shots, as if instinct and preparation had settled into something seamless, something almost inevitable under pressure. But the numbers alone do not explain why this innings felt different. Behind the aggression was a young cricketer who has clearly grown up with more than just ambition. He has grown up with responsibility, with gratitude, and with the kind of pressure that shapes character long before it shapes an athlete. Watch A Father's Pride: How RR, Dravid & Vikram Shaped Vaibhav Suryavanshi “main jo bhi hoon yahan apne parents ki wajah se hoon” His words were simple, but they landed with force. “Main jo bhi hoon yahan par apne parents ki wajah se hoon,” he said, and in that moment, the story widened beyond cricket. He spoke of his mother waking up at 2 a.m. for the household, sleeping only a few hours, and still cooking for him so he could leave for practice. He spoke of his father stepping away from work. He spoke of his elder brother taking over his father’s duties so the family could keep the home running. There was no polished speech, no carefully packaged line for the cameras. It was raw, honest and deeply personal. That honesty gave the knock a heartbeat. A family built on sacrifice What Vaibhav described is the kind of sacrifice many sporting dreams are built on, though it is often forgotten once the spotlight arrives. For every young player who walks out looking composed and fearless, there is usually a family making quiet adjustments behind the curtain. In the case of Vaibhav, it seems that the adjustments made by his family were truly significant and quite profound. His mother had to sacrifice her sleep, getting far less rest than she normally would. His father found it necessary to halt his work to provide support. Meanwhile, his brother stepped up and took on a portion of the responsibilities. Throughout all these challenges, the family maintained a steadfast belief that their struggles held a deeper purpose and meaning. That unwavering belief is crucial. This is because, in countless households, remarkable talent often goes unnoticed and unappreciated. What really transforms that latent potential into a tangible reality is not only the presence of skill, but also the enthusiasm and willingness of those around to make space for it to flourish. Hard work, faith and the weight of a dream Vaibhav’s reflection also carried a line that many will remember: that God sees the effort of those who work hard, and that failure does not belong to those who keep pushing forward. It was not just a statement of faith. It was a glimpse into the mindset of a player who understands that success is rarely instant, and never truly individual. His innings may have lasted only minutes. The making of it has taken years. That is why this knock resonated so widely. It was not merely explosive batting from a promising talent. It was the visible result of invisible labour. A mother’s sacrifice. A father’s pause. A brother’s responsibility. A son’s dream. Poll What do you think is more important for a young athlete's success? More than a scorecard moment Cricket enthusiasts will undoubtedly remember the extraordinary feat of scoring 50 runs in just 15 balls. Fans are sure to relive the amazing shots played, the breathtaking tempo witnessed, and the remarkable ease with which he tore apart the bowling attack. Yet, perhaps the most enduring memory associated with this achievement will be the powerful emotion that surrounded the moment. It serves as a poignant reminder that behind some of the most spectacular athletic performances are devoted families who have sacrificed everything without ever seeking recognition or applause. Vaibhav Suryavanshi did not merely execute a courageous innings; rather, he narrated a touching story filled with love, perseverance, and unshakeable belief. And perhaps, that emotional narrative is the most significant aspect of all.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Telangana TS SSC result 2026 to be released tomorrow: How to check, what to expect and what comes next - The Times of India
body: The Office of the Director of Government Examinations (DGE) Telangana will declare the Telangana SSC Class 10 results tomorrow, April 29, 2026 at 2 pm. Students will be able to check their results on official websites including results.bsetelangana.org, results.bse.telangana.gov.in, bse.telangana.gov.in and school.edu.telangana.gov.in. The results will be announced by K. Keshava Rao, Minister to the Government of Telangana. Senior officials including Dr. Yogita Rana, Indian Administrative Service (IAS), Principal Secretary to Government, and Dr. E. Naveen Nicolas, IAS, Director of School Education, will be present at the announcement event in Hyderabad. Students can also access their results through the MeeSeva WhatsApp service on 8096958096. Exam overview and participation The TS SSC Public examinations 2026 were conducted from March 14 to April 16 across Telangana. A total of 5,28,239 students appeared for the Class 10 board exams. The exams were held at around 2,676 centres across the state. A 24×7 control room was set up at the Office of the Director of Government Examinations in Hyderabad to assist students during the examination period. To pass the TS SSC exams, students must secure a minimum of 35 per cent marks in each subject. Steps to check and download TS SSC Class 10 result 2026 Students can follow these steps to access and download their TS SSC)Class 10 result once it is released: - Visit any of the official websites: results.bsetelangana.org, results.bse.telangana.gov.in, bse.telangana.gov.in or school.edu.telangana.gov.in - Click on the link for “TS SSC Class 10 Result 2026” on the homepage - Enter the required login details such as hall ticket number in the given field - Submit the details to view the result on the screen - Check all details mentioned in the marks memo carefully - Download the result and take a printout for future reference Students can also check their result through the MeeSeva WhatsApp service by sending a message to 8096958096 and following the instructions.Result timeline in recent years The declaration schedule has remained close to the end of April in recent cycles. In 2025, the TS SSC results were announced on April 30 at 2:15 pm. The exams were conducted from March 21 to April 4. The overall pass percentage of regular candidates stood at 92.78 per cent. Girls recorded a pass rate of 94.26 per cent, while boys stood at 91.32 per cent. In 2024, the results were also declared on April 30. A total of 5,05,813 students appeared, of whom 4,91,862 passed. In 2023, the results were declared later on May 10. The overall pass percentage was 86.60 per cent. Girls recorded a pass rate of 88.53 per cent, higher than boys. In 2022, the results were announced on June 29. Around 5 lakh students appeared, and the overall pass percentage was 90 per cent. Recounting and re-verification process After the results are declared, students who are not satisfied with their marks can apply for recounting or re-verification. Last year, the recounting process required a fee of Rs. 500 per subject and students were asked to apply for it within 15 days of result declaration. Applications were submitted to the Office of the Director of Government Examinations, Telangana, for the same. For re-verification, students applied through their school headmaster. The fee was Rs. 1,000 per subject. The process includes checking for unmarked answers and verifying totals. It does not include reassessment of answers. Supplementary examinations Students who do not pass in one or more subjects will be able to appear for the Telangana SSC Advanced Supplementary examinations. Last year, these exams were conducted from June 3 to June 13 in the morning session from 9:30 am to 12:30 pm. Candidates were advised to complete fee payment within the prescribed schedule without waiting for recounting or re-verification results. A similar schedule is expected to be followed this year.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Melania Trump: 'Enough is enough': Trump, Melania call on ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel - what triggered the row - The Times of India
body: - News - World News - US News - 'Enough is enough': Trump, Melania call on ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel - what triggered the row Trending After the White House correspondents’ dinner shooting, Jimmy Kimmel came under criticism for a rather insensitive comment towards Melania Trump after he referred to her as an “expectant widow” during a parody monologue aired days before the incident.The remark was made during a skit in which Kimmel pretended to host the event while discussing the Trumps’ connections to Jeffrey Epstein. In the segment, he said, “Our first lady Melania is here. So beautiful, Mrs Trump, you have a glow like an expectant widow.” Melania Trump condemned the comment in a post on X, saying, “Kimmel’s hateful and violent rhetoric is intended to divide our country. His monologue about my family isn’t comedy- his words are corrosive and deepens the political sickness within America. People like Kimmel shouldn’t have the opportunity to enter our homes each evening to spread hate.“A coward, Kimmel hides behind ABC because he knows the network will keep running cover to protect him. Enough is enough. It is time for ABC to take a stand. How many times will ABC’s leadership enable Kimmel’s atrocious behavior at the expense of our community.” Donald Trump later weighed in, calling for ABC to fire the comedian. At a news conference, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt also criticised Kimmel's remarks, saying, "This kind of rhetoric about the president, the first lady and his supporters is completely deranged.”Kimmel has since responded to the criticism and calls for his firing. On Monday’s episode of “Jimmy Kimmel Live!”, he said: “You know how sometimes you wake up in the morning and the First Lady puts out a statement demanding you be fired from your job? We’ve all been there. Right?”He explained that his “expectant widow” remark was “a joke about their age difference and the look of joy we see on [Melania’s] face” when she is with the president, adding that it was not meant as a call for violence.“It was a very light roast joke about the fact that he’s almost 80 and she’s younger than I am. It was not by any stretch of the definition a call to assassination. And they know that,” he said.Kimmel added, “I’ve been very vocal for many years speaking out against gun violence, in particular. But I understand that the First Lady had a stressful experience over the weekend. And probably every weekend is pretty stressful in that house. And also, I agree that hateful and violent rhetoric is something we should reject. I do. And I think a great place to start to dial that back would be to have a conversation with your husband about it.” The backlash follows a shooting near the correspondents’ dinner at the Washington Hilton, where the Trumps were attending. They were evacuated safely, and Donald Trump later described the evening as “a rather traumatic experience” for his wife.The suspect, identified as 31-year-old Cole Tomas Allen, was tackled by agents near a staircase leading down to a ballroom where hundreds of journalists, officials and public figures were attending the event. He appeared in court on Monday and was charged with attempting to assassinate the president.Some Republicans also weighed in on Kimmel before and after the incident. Meghan McCain wrote on X, “I am so deeply sick of Jimmy Kimmel and the hate he spreads in the world.”Kimmel has previously faced controversy over his comments. Last September, ABC suspended him after remarks about the shooting of conservative influencer Charlie Kirk, in which he said the "Maga gang" was trying to "score political points" from the murder. Poll Do you believe Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for his remarks? End of Article Follow Us On Social Media

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Netflix unveils ‘VOID’, an AI model that can change a movie plot | - The Times of India
body: Netflix has unveiled an AI model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, Netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next, essentially altering the causality. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background. VOID goes a step further: after erasing an object from a scene, it calculates how the surrounding environment should realistically respond to its absence, and in the process it adjusts the behaviour of other elements in the frame accordingly. The model, called VOID, is a vision-language system designed to edit video footage with a level of contextual awareness that sets it apart from several existing tools. For example, imagine this scenario: A movie director has just wrapped up a multi-million dollar action sequence where the star drives straight into a fiery, head-on collision. The cars explode, debris scatters, and the scene is a wrap. But suddenly, there is request to change the scene: The character survives and drive off into the sunset instead. This would mean costly reshoots or millions spent on computer-generated graphics. How Netflix VOID works Netflix’s VOID can alter the scene and digitally erase the collision, the second vehicle, the smoke, and the flying debris. It will then generate new, realistic footage of pristine pavement, making it look as though the main character simply drove down an empty, peaceful road. The AI’s ‘magic’ lies in its grasp of real-world physics. Let us understand this by a different example. If a video shows a person jumping into a pool and splashing water onto the deck, VOID can completely erase the person. It will then intelligently alter the water and the wet concrete, leaving behind a perfectly still, undisturbed pool as if nobody had ever jumped in. “To train the model, we generate a new paired dataset of counterfactual object removals using Kubric and HUMOTO, where removing an object requires altering downstream physical interactions. During inference, a vision-language model identifies regions of the scene affected by the removed object. These regions are then used to guide a video diffusion model that generates physically consistent counterfactual outcomes,” Netflix said. “Experiments on both synthetic and real data show that our approach better preserves consistent scene dynamics after object removal compared to prior video object removal methods,” the company added. Netflix isn’t keeping this powerful tool locked behind studio doors, and made the VOID model available to the public on the AI platform Hugging Face, allowing anyone to download and use it. While there are already several video-altering tools on the market, such as Runway, DiffuEraser, and ProPainter, the Netflix team claims VOID is vastly superior, as per The Register.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Exit poll results 2026 for Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, Puducherry: Date, time, where, and how to watch | - The Times of India
body: NEW DELHI: With the high-stakes second and final phase of the West Bengal assembly elections 2026 concluding on Wednesday, all eyes are now on exit poll projections that are expected to be released after 6.30 pm tomorrow, in line with Election Commission rules. Voting in Phase 2 will cover the remaining constituencies of the 294-seat Assembly, and is up for an intense political activity across districts, with both the ruling Trinamool Congress (TMC) and the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) making last-minute appeals to voters amid a closely watched contest. Watch Inside Bengal Phase 2: Why South Bengal could shift the 2026 election balance According to Election Commission guidelines, exit polls are strictly prohibited until 30 minutes after polling ends in the final phase. ECI will allow 2026 exit poll results after 6.30 pm on April 29. You can track exit polls for West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry here.Strong turnout in Phase 1 sets the tone In West Bengal, voting was held in 152 constituencies on April 23 during the first phase of the elections, registering a record turnout of nearly 93% in several regions. The high participation levels have added to the anticipation surrounding final results. Voter turnout in other states Bengal recorded a historic 93.2% turnout in the first phase of assembly elections, while Tamil Nadu's final provisional figure settled at 85.1% after a marginal revision, EC data showed on Saturday. In Assam, polling for all 126 seats concluded with a final and historic turnout of 85.5%, reflecting strong voter participation across rural and urban belts. In Kerala, voting across 140 constituencies concluded with a final turnout of approximately 75%–78%, indicating steady participation in a tightly contested multi-front election. In Puducherry, an unprecedented voter turnout of 89.87 per cent was recorded in yesterday’s voting for the Assembly Elections. As per the final figures released by the Election Commission, it is the highest ever recorded polling percentage in the Union Territory since its merger with India and the first Assembly election held in 1964. The final counting of votes across all poll-bound states will take place on May 4. Track and compare exit poll projections here.Elections 2026 mark a pivotal year for democratic processes across various regions in India and globally. This includes key state assembly elections, local body polls, and by-elections that could significantly impact national-level politics. Get real-time updates, important dates, voting procedures, and verified news — all in one place. Whether you're tracking results or exploring candidate profiles, this is your go-to hub for Elections 2026.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: West Bengal polls: With CM Mamata Banerjee, PM Narendra Modi leading charge, candidates take heat in stride for final weekend voter outreach | Kolkata News - The Times of India
body: KOLKATA: Politicians across party lines made a beeline to make the most of the last Sunday of campaigning before the second phase of polling. While several started early to finish by 11 am, some braved the heat even after 12 noon. Though the scorching sun was missing in morning, there was extreme discomfort throughout the day. Candidates, irrespective of party affiliation, started early to cover the maximum area while interacting with voters. Most were seen wearing light-coloured cotton clothes to avoid discomfort. Chief minister Mamata Banerjee led Trinamool campaign, addressing four public meetings and one ‘padayatra', all in her home constituency Bhowanipore. She remained on the move through Sunday evening. Trinamool national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee addressed three back-to-back rallies in East Burdwan and South 24 Parganas. Ghatal MP and actor Dev joined several roadshows in North 24 Parganas, while actor Subhashree campaigned in Barrackpore for TMC candidate and her husband, director Raj Chakraborty. The BJP campaign was spearheaded by PM Narendra Modi, who addressed two rallies, while Union home minister Amit Shah held rallies and joined roadshows deep into the evening. BJP president Nitin Nabin also attended campaign events, including a Salt Lake roadshow. In Beleghata, Trinamool candidate Kunal Ghosh held a colourful rally marked by white and blue balloons and Dhamsa-Madol performances along Raja Dinendra Street, Vivekananda Road, Rajendralal Street and Canal West Road. He greeted voters and interacted with residents during the march. "Today I am campaigning in my home constituency. Though I am busy in my own assembly constituency, I can say that the people of Bengal want Mamata Di again, and the wave is indicating the same message. The development people have seen during Mamata Banerjee's regime, and the BJP's agency politics and deletion of citizens' names in the name of SIR in Bengal, will not be accepted. Every minute and every second, the wave in support of Mamata Banerjee has been increasing. I need not put in extra effort for this election as I already have a strong connection with the people of this constituency, and I know they will vote for the TMC," he said. Trinamool candidate Bratya Basu began his South Dum Dum campaign at 8 am, covering Kalindi and nearby areas. Nayna Bandyopadhyay, TMC candidate in Chowringhee, visited Baithakkhana Bazar, interacting with fish sellers and buyers early in the morning. BJP opposition leader Suvendu Adhikari, who is fighting from Bhawanipore assembly constituency, began his morning campaign at Potua Para, where he offered puja at a temple. He met locals, conducted a door-to-door campaign and spoke to residents. In the evening, he carried out another door-to-door campaign at Sadar Street, where he campaigned with a replica of an EVM machine to show voters which button to press for his victory. Rakesh Singh, the BJP candidate from Kolkata Port, held a roadshow from Mudiali, Bandhanbartalla, Fateh More to Albester More. State minister Sashi Panja opted for door-to-door meetings in apartment clusters and residential pockets, focusing on Bagbazar and adjoining areas. Her schedule, however, was disrupted by the PM's roadshow route. "There were pockets left to be covered, areas where I wanted to reach out on foot rather than from a vehicle. Bagbazar was my focus area today. But things did not go exactly as planned. Today, two of my programmes were planned close to the PM's route. The evening plan got cancelled, and even the morning one was restricted. But I will maintain courtesy as he is the PM," Panja said. In Jorasanko, Trinamool nominee Vijay Upadhyay led a roadshow with supporters carrying party umbrellas. "The idea is not just to beat the heat. It is also to show that Didi is our umbrella," he said. In Behala West, BJP's Indranil Khan held a roadshow through Sarsuna and Parnasree. CPM candidate Dipsita Dhar in Dum Dum Uttar attended a youth-focused discussion and a pet lovers' meet after a rally with Md Salim and actor Sreelekha Mitra.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers | - The Times of India
body: AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers. “When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report. He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration. Massive investments, growing risks Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals. Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.” Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds. Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation. Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.” He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Rohit Sharma injury update: MI keep fans guessing ahead of SRH clash at Wankhede | Cricket News - The Times of India
body: MUMBAI: The availability of Rohit Sharma remains uncertain ahead of Mumbai Indians’ crucial IPL 2026 clash against Sunrisers Hyderabad, with the franchise confirming that a final decision will be taken only at the toss on Wednesday. “Rohit has continued to progress but any call on his involvement in Wednesday's game will be taken at the toss,” an MI spokesperson was quoted as saying by news agency PTI on Tuesday, keeping fans and team management on edge ahead of the Wankhede encounter. Watch Virat Kohli inaugurates his childhood coach Rajkumar Sharma's new academy Rohit Sharma’s recovery under watch The former MI skipper has been sidelined since April 12 after suffering a hamstring injury during a home game against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Since then, Rohit has been steadily working his way back, featuring in multiple net sessions in recent days. At the Wankhede Stadium on Tuesday, the 38-year-old was present during training but refrained from batting. Instead, he focused on light warm-ups and running drills under the supervision of support staff, followed by an extended discussion on the sidelines before leaving the field. While his gradual progress is a positive sign, MI appear cautious about rushing him back into match action. Suryakumar leads intense preparations In Rohit’s potential absence, the spotlight has shifted to Suryakumar Yadav, who put in an extended training session on the eve of the match. The dynamic batter was the first to arrive at the nets and trained for nearly 90 minutes, signalling his intent to rediscover top form amid a packed schedule. The session also saw interactions between players from both camps, with Suryakumar later seen in conversation with Abhishek Sharma and Ishan Kishan, while MI head coach Mahela Jayawardene exchanged words with SRH opener Travis Head. With Rohit’s participation hanging in the balance, MI will be hoping for a timely boost as they prepare for a key contest at home.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: 'Insecurity kisi ki nahi hai': Paresh Rawal opens up on his 25-year bond with Akshay Kumar amid 'Bhoot Bangla' trailer release | Hindi Movie News - The Times of India
body: In an industry often marred by reports of massive egos and professional rivalries, veteran actor Paresh Rawal has shed light on the refreshing work culture shared with his long-time collaborator, Akshay Kumar. Amid the trailer release of Priyadarshan’s upcoming horror-comedy 'Bhooth Bangla', Rawal addressed the seamless chemistry that has defined their decades-long partnership in an interview recently. Dismissing any notions of competition, the actor emphasized that despite Akshay’s global stardom, there is a profound sense of security and honesty on set. Paresh Rawal on working with Akshay Kumar Speaking about his current collaboration with Akshay Kumar and director Priyadarshan on the sets of 'Bhooth Bangla', Rawal opened up to Moneycontrol on what makes their work together so seamless. Watch Comedy Classic Revival: Paresh Rawal Confirms Hera Pheri 3 With Iconic Babu Rao Rawal stated, "Insecurity kisi ki nahi hai; Akshay (Kumar), itna bada star hone ke baad bhi, insecurity nahi hai. Koi meri peeth mein chura maar dega, aisa kisi ko nahi lagta hai. Araam se kaam hota hai and with honesty." (No one has insecurity, not even Akshay, despite being such a big star. No one thinks someone's going to stab them in the back. Work happens smoothly and with honesty.) Rawal further credited Priyadarshan’s leadership for fostering a generous spirit among the cast. He said, "And all the actors under Priyan Ji's guidance and direction are very generous and charitable towards each other. Toh chemistry banti hai woh bahut hi achcha banta hai." Paresh Rawal gives an update on 'Hera Pheri 3' For over a year, 'Hera Pheri 3' has been embroiled in a rollercoaster, ranging from casting changes to legal disputes. However, Paresh Rawal has now put all speculation to rest. During the same conversation, Rawal said "Don't listen to anything; Hera Pheri 3 is coming. I will start shooting for it soon." The statement directly addresses the swirl of speculation, delay reports, and conflicting updates that have surrounded the project since it was first announced. For fans who have followed the franchise's troubled production journey through Paresh Rawal's reported exit, Akshay Kumar's alleged Rs. 25 crore damages case, and the eventual resolution of both, hearing the actor himself confirm an imminent shoot start is about as reassuring as it gets. The original trio of Akshay Kumar, Suniel Shetty, and Paresh Rawal is set to reprise their iconic roles with Priyadarshan directing the third chapter just as he helmed the beloved 2000 original. Paresh Rawal on the work front Before 'Hera Pheri 3' hits floors, Akshay Kumar, Priyadarshan, Paresh Rawal and Rajpal Yadav have united in 'Bhooth Bangla'. The film is scheduled for a release on April 17, 2026. The horror comedy also stars Tabu, Mithila Palkar, the late Asrani and Wamiqa Gabbi in pivotal roles.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Who Is Ajay Pal Sharma: Who is Ajay Pal Sharma? UP IPS officer with 500+ encounters who went viral after Bengal poll warning video | Lucknow News - The Times of India
body: LUCKNOW: UP IPS officer Ajay Pal Sharma has emerged as a talking point during the ongoing West Bengal assembly elections 2026 after a video of him issuing a stern warning to a political functionary went viral. Who is IPS officer Ajay Pal Sharma? A 2011-batch Indian Police Service (IPS) officer, Ajay Pal Sharma is known for his aggressive and action-oriented policing style. A former dental surgeon, he left his medical profession to join the civil services and has since served in several districts of Uttar Pradesh, including Jaunpur, Shamli, Rampur, and Hathras. Watch Bengal Poll: TMC Files HC Petition Alleging Observer Met BJP Candidate, Raises Neutrality Concerns Currently posted as assistant commissioner of police (Law and Order) in Prayagraj, Sharma has built a reputation as a tough officer with a strong focus on law enforcement. During his tenure as superintendent of police in Jaunpur, he was credited with leading over 500 police encounters, including a record 136 encounters in just 22 months. The warning was reportedly directed at supporters of Trinamool Congress candidate Jahangir Khan, who has been accused by locals of intimidation. Khan, considered close to TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee, has been at the centre of controversy. Sources said Sharma initially faced resistance while trying to trace Khan’s residence, as local residents and even some police personnel were reluctant to share details, allegedly due to fear. However, his team eventually located the address after a search operation. Sharma also flagged irregularities in security deployment. While Khan is entitled to ‘Y-category’ security with 10 personnel, 14 policemen were found deployed at his residence. Following this, Sharma issued a notice to the local SP seeking an explanation for the excess deployment of personnel.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Mbose Sslc Result: MBOSE Class 10th SSLC result 2026 to release tomorrow: When and where to check - The Times of India
body: MBOSE SSLC result 2026: The Meghalaya Board of School Education (MBOSE) has announced that the Secondary School Leaving Certificate (SSLC) Examination 2026 results will be declared tomorrow, April 7, 2026, at 11 AM. The board’s official notification from its Tura headquarters emphasized that the results will be available exclusively online. No physical display of scorecards will be made at the MBOSE offices in Tura or Shillong. T. R. Laloo, Director Accreditation and Controller of Examinations, issued the official notice confirming that the MBOSE SSLC results 2026 will be accessible only through the board’s online platforms. Students can download their results directly from the following official websites: - www.mbose.in - www.mboseresults.in - www.megresults.nic.in MBOSE SSLC result 2026: Steps to download scorecards Candidates can follow the steps elucidated here to download the MBOSE SSLC result 2026 - Go to any of the official MBOSE result websites. - Navigate to the “Results” section and click on the SSLC Result 2026 link. - Enter your roll number and date of birth in the required fields. - Submit the details to view your scorecard on the screen. After the MBOSE SSLC Result 2026 is declared, students who qualify can begin preparing for their higher secondary (Class 11) admissions. They will need to select streams such as Science, Commerce, or Arts based on their interests and marks obtained. Schools across Meghalaya will start the admission process shortly, and students should keep their scorecards ready for verification and registration at their preferred institutions. Aspirants are advised to stay tuned to the official website to get the complete details of the MBOSE SSLC result 2026.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Sonali Bendre on Cancer battle, says early detection could have changed everything: 'It didn’t have to reach stage four' | Hindi Movie News - The Times of India
body: Actor Sonali Bendre, who was diagnosed with stage 4 metastatic cancer in 2018, has once again spoken about her journey and the lessons it brought. After undergoing intensive treatment in New York, including chemotherapy and surgery, she emerged stronger and continues to use her voice to raise awareness around the illness. Opening up about where she found the strength to fight, Sonali shared, “I had a great support system, and that was my strength. I am sure there are a lot of people who are transparent about it. I just happened to be more well-known than them.” She also recalled how speaking publicly about her illness made her realise how little people openly discussed cancer. Watch Sonali Bendre Hits Back At Backlash Over Favouring Autophagy! Sonali Bendre breaking silence around Cancer Highlighting the stigma that once surrounded the disease, she said, “It was a taboo subject, and I did not realise it. They were mentioning this as the C disease. They wouldn't even name it.” Sonali emphasised how awareness and open conversations were missing when she needed them the most. Reflecting on her diagnosis, the actor admitted that things could have been different with timely detection. “What I realised was, for me, I needn't have reached the fourth stage. Had I tested and detected it early, I needn't have gone through the hardship that I went through,” she said. She also underlined that cancer, when caught early, is treatable and “not a death sentence.” Sonali believes her public platform helped push a much-needed conversation forward. “That's what I realised: that when I am in the public eye, maybe that's what I can give back. I can bring about a conversation around it,” she said, adding that the change in awareness over the past seven years has been significant. She expressed satisfaction over the growing awareness, saying, “The way people are talking about it now, the way they are getting tested, it was not the case before. That I am happy about; I feel like it is the only thing I have done in my life.” Her journey, she believes, has contributed to encouraging more people to prioritise early detection. Sonali Bendre's work front Now cancer-free after achieving remission, Sonali continues to stay active professionally and personally. She was last seen in the web series ‘The Broken News’ (2024) and also runs a book club while hosting ‘The Happy Pawdcast’, a podcast centred on pet care and animal welfare. Disclaimer: This article contains mention of cancer and personal experiences related to the disease, which readers may find sensitive. The information shared is based on personal accounts or reported events and is for general awareness only. It is not intended to be a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of a qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: NHL overtime rules in playoffs explained with no shootouts and sudden death format shaping intense 2026 postseason results | NHL News - The Times of India
body: The NHL playoffs have once again shown the power of the overtime rule 2026. It is to make or break big games. Playoff hockey is delivering wild, extended battles that are far from regular-season formats, with numerous sudden-death endings already seen. Playoff overtime, unlike regular-season overtime, can end at any moment, and it is a test of endurance and accuracy for a winner. This format has already affected a few key outcomes, and the discussions of the NHL news roundup are focusing heavily on the overtime drama. Watch Extra Overtime Rules in the NHL? #nhlphd #NHL #hockey #Overtime #funfacts How NHL playoff overtime rules work with the 5-on-5 sudden-death format in 2026 games NHL playoff overtime is a sudden-death, 5-on-5 format where the first goal wins the game. Full 20-minute periods are played after a regular intermission until a winner is decided. Many 2026 NHL match results have seen dramatic finishes as a result of this structure. Teams go on a 15-minute break and then come back onto the ice. If no goal is scored, more overtime periods keep going with the same format. Zero shootout gameplay takes precedence over skill challenges. Recent NHL updates show this format has proven so impactful. In other games, Anaheim Ducks’ Ryan Poehling scored a sudden-death goal early, and Vegas Golden Knights’ Shea Theodore scored a late overtime winner. Wyatt Johnston of the Dallas Stars added another double OT goal, showing that endurance is tested in extended play. No shootouts, so all the emphasis is on team depth and adjustments during the game. Matt Boldy of the Minnesota Wild and Lane Hutson of the Montreal Canadiens are players who have taken advantage of those opportunities and turned tight games into series-defining wins. Looking from an NHL game preview perspective, teams are now preparing for longer games, rotating lines efficiently and maintaining defensive discipline, as coaches stress the need to avoid errors, as a single mistake can immediately kill the game. The NHL has seen marathon overtime games in the past, with multiple periods, and the 2026 postseason is keeping that tradition alive. The current format hits a good balance between fairness and maximizing the level of competition, in line with the league’s emphasis on decisive results. NHL trade rumors, team strategies are changing, and overtime play is becoming a major factor in postseason success. The 2026 playoffs revealed that any team that wants to advance has to be able to handle sudden-death situations.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: "No longer trusts Blake or Ryan": Blake Lively “still hopeful” despite Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s firm wedding decision | NFL News - The Times of India
body: Speculation about the guest list for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s long-awaited wedding is already swirling and not everyone is expected to get an invite. What once appeared to be a rock-solid union between Swift and Blake Lively now appears to be strained, and sources say the fallout could have real repercussions for the big day. Behind the glitz of a high-profile wedding there’s a quieter story of trust, distance and shifting loyalties. The tension is due to the Justin Baldoni legal controversy, with private exchanges allegedly surfacing and Swift feeling exposed. Since then, her inner circle has tightened. As invitations begin circulating, the absence of certain longtime friends is becoming just as notable as the star-studded names expected to attend. Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding: A guest list shaped by trust, not history Sources close to the situation claim Swift has drawn a firm line when it comes to her wedding day. According to journalist Rob Shuter, the decision is already settled. “They're not invited. Period,” one source confirmed, adding, “Taylor wants a drama-free day and no longer trusts Blake or Ryan.” Another person close to the singer insisted the decision had been made some time ago: “ It's not a question of 'will she or won't she.' She won't.” The reported exclusion also extends to Ryan Reynolds, Lively’s husband. For Swift, the focus appears to be on preserving a calm and private celebration rather than revisiting complicated relationships. Those familiar with the situation say the singer felt deeply unsettled by how events unfolded during the Baldoni case, especially with her name being pulled into the spotlight. Blake Lively's hope lingers despite the silence While the door seems closed from Swift’s side, Lively is said to be holding on to optimism. According to Substack, she still believes their shared history carries weight. “Blake is totally optimistic,” the source says. “She still thinks about the beginning. She was there when Taylor and Travis started, and she feels like she’s part of their story.” That sentiment reflects a different perspective, one rooted in nostalgia and emotional ties. Yet, another insider offered a more grounded take: “Blake is still holding out hope ,” but she may be the only one, “she believes that invitation is on its way.” As the wedding draws closer, the contrast is striking. One side seeks peace and closure. The other quietly waits, hoping the past still counts for something.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Taylor Swift files trademarks for voice, image to counter AI deepfakes: Report - The Times of India
body: Taylor Swift has reportedly filed trademark applications for her voice and image. According to a report by news agency Reuters, Swift has filed applications with the US Patent and Trademark Office last week, seeking trademark for two audio clips and one image of herself. The move, the report said, is aimed at protecting her likeness from misuse by artificial intelligence (AI). Swift’s image and voice have previously been used in many AI-generated deepfakes, including fake ads, false political endorsements and explicit content. Taylor Swift trademarks two audio clips and a photo As stated in the Reuters report, Taylor Swift’s applications include two audio clips and a photo. The applications list Swift's TAS Rights Management as being the owner of the audio clips and images. In one clip, Swift says: “Hey, it's Taylor Swift, and you can listen to my new album, ‘The Life of a Showgirl,’ on demand on Amazon Music Unlimited.” The second clip says: “Hey, it’s Taylor. My brand new album ‘The Life of a Showgirl’ is out on October 3 and you can click to presave it so you can listen to it on Spotify.” The image, on the other hand, included in the filing shows the pop star performing on stage with a guitar. Trademark attorney Josh Gerben, cited by the news agency, said the filings are “specifically designed to protect Taylor from threats posed by artificial intelligence.” He added that trademarks could provide an additional layer of protection alongside existing laws. Not a first The report noted that similar filings have been made by other public figures. Actor Matthew McConaughey has also taken steps to protect his voice and likeness. In January, Conaughey told The Wall Street Journal that “we want to create a clear perimeter around ownership with consent and attribution the norm in an AI world.” "Historically, singers relied on copyright law to protect their recorded music," Gerben wrote as cited in the Reuters report. "But AI technologies now allow users to generate entirely new content that mimics an artist’s voice without copying an existing recording, creating a gap that trademarks may help fill."

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Austin Reaves injury update: Latest on Lakers star’s Game 5 status | NBA News - The Times of India
body: Austin Reaves injury update arrives at a crutial moment for the Los Angeles Lakers. A sweep slipped away after a flat Game 4 loss to the Houston Rockets. The 115-96 defeat exposed gaps in ball control and shot creation. LeBron James finished with just 10 points, while Deandre Ayton’s ejection broke any rhythm the team had left. Now the series moves forward with fresh urgency. The Lakers still lead 3-1, but the missed chance has shifted attention to who can return. According to Shams Charania, Reaves has a real shot at playing in Game 5. In contrast, Luka Doncic is ruled out for the rest of the series, making Reaves’ status even more important. Austin Reaves game 5 injury update The Austin Reaves game 5 injury update points to cautious optimism. The guard has been out since April 2 with a Grade 2 oblique strain, yet his status has quietly improved. He has been listed as questionable in recent games and even went through warmups, a sign that his return is getting close. Insider Brett Siegel shared a positive outlook on his recovery. “I would tend to believe the Lakers are feeling better about Austin Reaves' chances of being available for Game 5 in LA on Wednesday,” Siegel posted on X. “He has not had any setbacks over the last week, as the team has been comfortable easing him and not rushing him back with how this series has gone.” ESPN’s Shams Charania’s comment on his return also aligns with Siegel. Even if he suits up, expectations will stay measured. Reaves has missed weeks of action, and game rhythm takes time. Still, the Lakers only need one win to advance. A controlled return, even in limited minutes, could steady the offense and reduce pressure on James and the supporting cast. Reaves in the roster would certainly make a difference but rushing him into action could also be dangerous. What did JJ Redick say about Austin Reaves Head coach JJ Redick did not hide the team’s struggles without its key guards. After Game 4, he pointed to the missing pieces in clear terms. “That’s the biggest challenge we have is just the ball-handling and downhill drivers, not having those guys,” he said. That issue showed up in the numbers. The Lakers committed 24 turnovers, many coming at key moments. Without Reaves and Doncic, the offense looked slower and easier to disrupt. Yet, the first three games told a different story. Players like Marcus Smart, Luke Kennard, and Rui Hachimura stepped up, helping build the 3-0 lead. Game 5 now feels like a pivot point. Reaves could return at the right time, not to carry the team, but to restore balance. Houston, still without Kevin Durant, will try to extend the fight. The Lakers, meanwhile, are one steady performance away from moving on, with or without their full roster.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: 'It still haunts us': Pahalgam attack, one year on — scars speak between memory and recovery | India News - The Times of India
body: - News - India News - 'It still haunts us': Pahalgam attack, one year on — scars speak between memory and recovery Trending "Some boundaries should never be crossed."A year on, the Army’s words no longer stand alone. They echo as memory and warning — marking the day that altered how Pahalgam is seen, felt and remembered. It was a moment when the valley’s calm fractured without warning. Birdsong gave way to gunfire. Routine turned to panic. Twenty-six lives were lost — and with them, the sense of safety in a place once celebrated on India’s tourism map, now grappling with a violence it was never meant to hold.One year on, the scars are still visible. In the half-filled hotels that once thrived on summer crowds and in the quieter streets, where tourism suffered, the effects still linger. Security forces immediately launched search operations across surrounding forest belts, and the area was sealed off. In early 2026, further detentions were reported in connection with individuals suspected to be directly involved in the execution of the attack. Security officials involved in the investigation said these arrests helped map wider support chains, though legal proceedings remained ongoing.Alongside military and security responses, India launched a coordinated diplomatic outreach in the days following the attack, briefing foreign governments and multilateral partners on the incident and its cross-border terror links.The Indian diplomatic outreach saw seven all-party delegations visit over 30 countries and the European Union, holding high-level briefings across major global capitals in North America, Europe, West Asia, Africa, and Asia. The missions presented India’s position on the Pahalgam attack and its counter-terror response, framing it as part of a broader effort to internationalise concerns on cross-border terrorism.As Prime Minister Narendra Modi said while marking the anniversary, the victims “will never be forgotten”. Read more: PM Modi pays tribute to Pahalgam victims on first anniversaryMeanwhile, as the security counter measures came into place, a broader impact trend started emerging through mid-2025. Tourism activity in Pahalgam and adjoining areas dropped sharply. Hotel associations and local tourism operators reported widespread cancellations during the peak summer window. Recovery began gradually by late summer, but with shorter travel cycles and increased dependence on security advisories.The attack reshaped the region in more ways than one — none more visible than in its tourism economy. There was a sense of fear amongst the locals and especially the tourists. Pahalgam was under lockdown and in Srinagar the security increased. I was getting calls from some tourists who wanted to leave Pahalgam overnight and come to us in Srinagar but were unable to do so as Pahalagam was under lockdown. Aditya, a Srinagar resident I felt panicked at that time because such incidents had never happened before. It is 280 km away from my home. At that time, there were no restrictions or curfew, but we were in deep pain. We tried our best to oppose this heinous crime against humanity by posting on social media and through physical gatherings to mourn the loss of lives Irfan Ahmad, Kishtwar resident Talking about a year back, When I heard about the Pahalgam attack I was shocked and shattered and more than that I was afraid to learn about this terror attack, &amp;amp;amp;amp;amp;quot; adding that, “in the whole region everyone was seen escaping to save himself and to leave Srinagar by any means. Monica Shivek, a work experience teacher with DIET Lieutenant General (Retd.) DP Pandey, former commander of the 15 Corps, told TOI, that the counter-terror approach is “based on intelligence to neutralise key leaders” and also aims to ensure that “financial networks that fund terror and separatist organisations are exposed.” He highlighted that while terror incidents had declined over time, the Pahalgam attack remained an exception in an otherwise improved security environment.He further said that post-incident reviews typically lead to reinforcement of security infrastructure near tourist areas and a “focused approach towards anti-terror operations in higher reaches of J&K to deny safe areas.”Meanwhile, a senior Army official talked to TOI about sustaining the confidence in security and retaining the trust of people. “The response of the people over the past year speaks for itself. Immediately after Operation Sindoor, we witnessed a footfall of over 4.1 lakh yatris for the Shri Amarnath Ji Yatra, reflecting strong confidence of the people in the security architecture and the enduring appeal of Jammu & Kashmir as a premier tourist destination.He also mentioned 1.9 crore tourist visits to Jammu and Kashmir in the past year, including strong winter arrivals.The official also highlighted the Udhampur–Srinagar–Baramulla Rail Link as a key boost to connectivity and tourism, while adding that “the spirit of Kashmiriyat… was evident as the people of J&K stood firmly with the nation in the aftermath of the Pahalgam terror attack.” Poll What should be prioritized to improve tourism in Pahalgam? End of Article Follow Us On Social Media

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Raising “robot-proof” kids: Why creativity and curiosity matter more than ever | - The Times of India
body: - News - lifestyle - parenting - parenting-stories - Raising “robot-proof” kids: Why creativity and curiosity matter more than ever Trending As we move into an era where everything is dominated by artificial intelligence, there is a growing need to reevaluate what education entails. As much as schools are still concentrating on test scores and businesses favoring individuals with excellent qualifications, experts believe that such standards might not be sufficient to ready children for the future. It requires developing skills that are difficult for computers to match, including creativity and problem solving.Vivienne Ming is a theoretical neuroscientist who founded The Human Trust. She has extensively researched how human beings can develop amid the emergence of new technologies.As reported in MSN.com Ming opened up about the shift that parents and educators need to make includes transitioning from the learning process that involves repetition to one that builds practical skills. The fear of making mistakes should no longer be instilled in children; instead, they should learn how to embrace mistakes as an integral part of the learning experience. Parents can encourage their children to accept mistakes by simply having conversations at home about obstacles and failures.One of the most critical changes in terms of developing creativity in children is providing an atmosphere that promotes curiosity. Children benefit greatly when they are immersed in a stimulating environment where they are faced with a variety of thoughts and problems that require critical thinking and independent judgment. As time goes by, and the nature of jobs changes drastically, it is important to ensure that adaptability and critical thinking remain central. Instead of focusing on getting the right answers, it would be ideal to ensure that children feel confident enough to ask better questions.As we continue to move towards an increasingly technological era, so must the education of our children. However, mere academic knowledge will not suffice if our children are to be successful in the long term. What distinguishes a person from others is his capacity to think outside the box, to cope with situations, and to apply creativity when faced with a challenge. Developing an open mind and the curiosity to ask questions and look at things from various angles can go a long way in laying a strong base for the future. These days, when everything seems to be just a click away, the true challenge lies in comprehending and analyzing the information. End of Article Follow Us On Social Media

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Iran war risk: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warns of oil shocks, sticky inflation and higher interest rates - The Times of India
body: - News - Business News - International Business News - Iran war risk: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warns of oil shocks, sticky inflation and higher interest rates Trending JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has warned that the ongoing war in Iran could trigger oil and commodity price shocks, keeping inflation elevated and pushing interest rates higher than current market expectations, Reuters reported.The warning came in his annual letter to shareholders, a day after US President Donald Trump escalated pressure on Iran by threatening to target key infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened. "Now, because of the war in Iran, we additionally face the potential for significant ongoing oil and commodity price shocks, along with the reshaping of global supply chains, which may lead to stickier inflation and ultimately higher interest rates than markets currently expect," Dimon said, news agency Reuters quoted as saying. Dimon, who has led JPMorgan for two decades, also highlighted broader geopolitical risks, including the war in Ukraine, tensions in the Middle East and friction with China."The challenges we all face are significant," he added.He said it remains uncertain whether the Iran war will achieve US objectives, while warning that nuclear proliferation remains the biggest risk linked to Iran.Markets have already begun pricing in these risks, with expectations of interest rate cuts this year largely fading amid rising inflation concerns triggered by the conflict. End of Article Follow Us On Social Media

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce spare no expense on star studded wedding as they seek privacy ahead of their big day | NFL News - The Times of India
body: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce live a life full of luxury and it seems their wedding will be no less. As the high profile couple gear up for the biggest day of their lives, it seems the sky is the limit when it comes to spending money on their wedding. Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce go all out when it comes to their lavish wedding as rumors swirl about the millions they are spending A few hours ago, as per Closer Online, a source has revealed that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have a vision for their multi million dollar wedding. The source said, “Taylor didn’t want money to be a factor in planning these parties, so she and Travis have set a $10 million limit…” The source added, “However, they’ve made it clear to their wedding party that if more needs to be spent to ensure everything runs smoothly, that won’t be an issue.” As per Closer Online, the source continued, “Insiders say several private jets will be involved, alongside villa rentals, a large team of private staff to cater for guests and plenty of other luxury extras.” However, this does not come off shocking since the billionaire pop star, Taylor Swift, has a net worth of a whopping $2.1 billion. While the Kansas City Chiefs’ star player, Travis Kelce, has a lesser net worth, he is still a highly paid player in the league with multiple ventures of his own, including a restaurant with Patrick Mahomes, a hit podcast with his brother and more. Where are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tying the knot? For now, as per Page Six, the date for the biggest event of the year has been set for 3rd July as both Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have immersed themselves completely into planning their multi million dollar wedding. The wedding venue is rumored to be held in Manhattan as both Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce seek privacy for their lavish wedding. Over the last few days, the billionaire pop star has been spotted in and around New York as she has used umbrellas to escape cameras and be private. For now, billions of fans wait for the two to tie the knot.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: US-Iran War: A daring rescue Hollywood blockbuster is on its way. Till then, pick your favourite from these 10 films on bringing someone home against all odds | English Movie News - The Times of India
body: Life imitates art, they say. And some of our best moments of thrill and joy have come from Hollywood movies about rescue efforts. Be it Black Hawk Down, 13 Hours, Saving Private Ryan, Argo or The Martian. There are many more. But right after the daring extraction of an American pilot from Iran’s remote and dangerous mountain range Sunday, the urge is to go back and watch all the movies where a daring rescue has kept us all on the edge of our seats or sofas. X (formerly Twitter) was abuzz with #Nooneleftbehind last evening as the story broke. While the US-Iran war has had many twists and turns, this is worthy of the next Hollywood script and a blockbuster one at that. Rescue stories are powerful because they do not just have the thrill factor attached to it; but because of a four-lettered word that the world needs more of: hope. In a world where hope is dwindling, and fast, such movies are a catharsis whether or not a mission is fully successful (read no one dies) or it has a heft cost. So, if you want to watch Hollywood movies made on the subject, here are the top 10 movies you can choose from: Saving Private Ryan (1998) Poster of 1998 film Saving Private Ryan In World War II, Captain John Miller, played by Tom Hanks, leads a squad into enemy territory to retrieve one man: Private James Ryan, the last surviving brother of four soldiers. But the film’s real conflict lies beneath the mission. As the men inch closer to Ryan, they begin to question the arithmetic of war itself—how many lives are worth one? The brutality is unflinching, yet what lingers is the quiet moral exhaustion of soldiers forced to assign value to human life. Black Hawk Down (2001) Directed by Ridley Scott, this is war stripped of sentiment. Set during the 1993 Battle of Mogadishu, the film follows an elite US mission that spirals into chaos after two helicopters are downed. What unfolds is a relentless, claustrophobic 14-hour firefight. There are no grand speeches here—only confusion, fractured command, and the raw instinct to survive. The film captures how quickly precision warfare can dissolve into disorder. Captain Philips (2013) Again led by Tom Hanks, this modern maritime thriller is less about spectacle and more about tension. When Somali pirates hijack the Maersk Alabama in 2009, Captain Richard Phillips becomes both negotiator and shield for his crew. Opposite him is Muse, a young pirate driven by desperation rather than ideology. The film resists easy binaries—this is not just a story of heroism, but of global inequality colliding violently on open waters. 13 Hours: The Secret Soldiers of Benghazi (2016) Directed by Michael Bay, this retelling of the 2012 Benghazi attacks focuses on six private security contractors defending a US compound under siege. Unlike traditional war narratives, this is a story of improvisation in the absence of clear command. The politics remain in the background; what takes center stage is the endurance of men holding a collapsing perimeter through a long, uncertain night. Argo (2012) Directed by and starring Ben Affleck, Argo transforms the 1979 Iranian hostage crisis into a slow-burning thriller of deception. The rescue plan—posing as a fake Hollywood film crew—is as absurd as it is ingenious. Yet beneath the tension lies a deeper commentary on performance: in a volatile political landscape, survival often depends on who can construct the most convincing illusion. Mad Max: Fury Road (2015) This is not a conventional rescue film, but one that redefines the idea. In a dystopian wasteland, Max (Tom Hardy) and Furiosa (Charlize Theron) attempt to liberate enslaved women from a tyrant. Here, rescue is rebellion. The film’s relentless chase structure mirrors its thematic urgency—freedom is not negotiated, it is seized. Dunkirk (2017) Dunkirk is considered one of the best rescue movies ever made Christopher Nolan crafts a war film that feels almost abstract in its intensity. Set during the 1940 evacuation of Allied troops, it fractures time into three intersecting perspectives—land, sea, and air. There are no central heroes, only collective survival. The film suggests that sometimes, rescue is not a singular act of bravery, but a shared, almost anonymous effort. The Finest Hours (2016) Based on a true Coast Guard mission in 1952, this film leans into old-school heroism. When a tanker splits apart during a storm, a small rescue crew sets out into near-impossible conditions. The narrative is straightforward, but its power lies in scale: fragile human effort against an indifferent, overwhelming sea. Thirteen Lives (2022) Directed by Ron Howard, this recounting of the 2018 Thai cave rescue is a study in quiet precision. There are no dramatic shortcuts—just methodical planning, technical expertise, and extraordinary patience. The film stands out for demystifying heroism; what saves the day here is not bravado, but discipline and collaboration across borders. Extraction (2020) Non-stop action thriller, Extraction, starring Chris Hemsworth Starring Chris Hemsworth, this high-octane thriller relocates the rescue narrative to the chaos of Dhaka. Tyler Rake, a mercenary with little left to lose, is tasked with saving a kidnapped boy. The film blends kinetic action with emotional undercurrents—suggesting that even the most transactional missions can become deeply personal.
