author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: John Ternus: Apple may launch 10 products under new CEO John Ternus, compared to three in Tim Cook's era | - The Times of India
body: When John Ternus officially takes over as Apple’s CEO on September 1, he will be launching a string of products – a number which is said to be more than thrice as compared to what the Cupertino-based giant debuted under Tim Cook (Apple Watch, AirPods, and Vision Pro) during his 15-year tenure. According to a report by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman, Ternus is poised to oversee the debut of roughly 10 new product categories in the coming years in multiple categories. John Ternus first product launch may be foldable iPhone Less than two weeks after stepping into the role, Ternus is expected to host the unveiling of the first-ever foldable iPhone, which is poised to instantly become the face of Apple’s next big chapter. Priced at an expected $2,000 or more, the smartphone is designed to boost revenue and entice Android users who have used foldables for years. Under Ternus's direction, Apple focused on four key areas to beat the competition, including durability of the folding mechanism, invisible crease, iPad-style experience and peak performance. Beyond foldable iPhone: From robots to AI wearables Gurman says that during a recent company town hall, Ternus told employees that Apple is about to “change the world” again, calling the current pipeline the most exciting of his career. Beyond the foldable phone, here are the nine other categories expected to shape his legacy: AI-powered smart homeApple is moving into the home to challenge Ring and Nest. This includes a Smart Home Hub (a HomePod with a screen), a Tabletop Robot featuring a screen on a moving robotic arm for better video calls, and a new privacy-focused Security Camera. AI wearablesApple is said to be developing “camera-first” wearables to feed data into its AI systems. These include Smart Glasses to compete with Meta’s Ray-Bans, AirPods with cameras to give Siri “eyes”, and a Circular AI Pendant that can be worn as a necklace or pinned to a shirt. Computing and AR devicesThe “Ternus Era” will likely see the first Touch-Screen MacBook in late 2026. Further out, Apple is aiming for a lightweight Augmented Reality (AR) glasses (targeted for 2028-2030) that could eventually replace the iPhone entirely. A 20-inch foldable iPad is also in development, the report noted. While Ternus is a hardware master, he must now align the entire company, specifically the software teams, to fix Siri and ensure the AI “brains” match the purported new hardware lineup. Poll Which type of AI wearable would you consider purchasing?

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Dc Vs Rcb: DC batting collapse vs RCB: What is the lowest total in IPL history? | Cricket News - The Times of India
body: - News - Sports News - Cricket News - IPL News - DC batting collapse vs RCB: What is the lowest total in IPL history? Trending Royal Challengers Bengaluru pacers Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood put Delhi Capitals under huge pressure on Monday, reducing them to 13 for 6 in the powerplay during their IPL 2026 match.It became the lowest powerplay score in a full IPL game.Both fast bowlers took three wickets each as Delhi’s top order collapsed early and RCB took control of the match.Their 13 for 6 after six overs broke the previous record for the lowest powerplay total in a full IPL match. At one stage, Delhi were also in danger of being bowled out for the lowest total in IPL history. However, in the end DC were bowled out for 75. 1. Royal Challengers Bengaluru (49) vs Kolkata Knight Riders (2017)The current lowest total in IPL history still belongs to Royal Challengers Bengaluru, who were bowled out for 49 against Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2017.That collapse happened at Eden Gardens when RCB were chasing 132. Their batting lineup was dismissed in just 9.4 overs, and no batter managed to score 10 or more. Nathan Coulter-Nile, Chris Woakes and Colin de Grandhomme took three wickets each.2. Rajasthan Royals (58) vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru (2009)The second-lowest total in IPL history is 58 by Rajasthan Royals against RCB in IPL 2009.That match took place in South Africa during the opening weekend of the season. Anil Kumble led the attack with 5 wickets for 5 runs as Rajasthan were bowled out in 15.1 overs. Stay updated with the latest IPL Live Score, IPL news on Times of India. Follow the IPL Schedule, check the IPL Points Table, and track the race for the IPL Orange Cap and IPL Purple Cap. End of Article Follow Us On Social Media

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Sridhar Vembu: Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu’s open letter to Indians in America: Your choice would be between … | - The Times of India
body: Zoho founder Sridhar Vembu has shared an open letter addressed to Indians living in the United States. Posted on X (former ly known as Twitter), the letter urges “brothers and sisters from Bharat” to consider returning to India and contribute to the country’s growth. In the post, Vembu reflected on his own journey to the US decades ago and acknowledged the opportunities it provided. At the same time, he pointed to changing perceptions around immigration and jobs, saying that Indians abroad are increasingly caught in broader political debates. “You may think the next election will fix this, but your choice would be between people who hate our Bharatiya civilisation and people who hate civilisation itself. That is the "hard right" vs "woke left" battle. You are mere bystanders to that conflict,” Vembu wrote. In the post, he added that global respect for Indians will depend on India’s progress, especially in technology, and called on professionals overseas to bring their experience back to help build the country’s future. Read Sridhar Vembu’s open letter to Indians in US Open letter to Indians in America.--Dear brothers and sisters from Bharat:Like I did 37 years ago, you arrived in America with no money but with a good education and cultural heritage from Bharat. You achieved outstanding success. America was good to us. For that we must remain grateful - gratitude is our Bharatiya way.Yet today, a significant number of Americans, may be not the majority but not too far from it either, believe that Indians "take away" American jobs and our success in America was unfairly earned. You may think the next election will fix this, but your choice would be between people who hate our Bharatiya civilisation and people who hate civilisation itself. That is the "hard right" vs "woke left" battle. You are mere bystanders to that conflict.Meanwhile there is one thing that is true now and will be true in the future: the respect Indians command world-wide will substantially depend on the fortunes of India herself. If India remains poor, the woke left will give us moral lectures with pity and the hard right, different moral lectures with scorn ("hellhole") and we must not confuse either with respect.Respect in today's world, along with prosperity and security, comes from one source: a nation's technological prowess. India produces sufficient brain power to achieve that prowess but alas we exported so much of that talent, particularly to America. As we develop that prowess in India, our civilisational strength will assert itself.As difficult as it is for many of you to contemplate this, please come back home. Bharat Mata needs your talent. Our vast youthful population needs the technology leadership you gained over the years to guide them towards prosperity. Let's do it with a missionary zeal.Respectfully Sridhar Vembu

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: IPL 2026: 10000 IPL runs for Virat Kohli? Former cricketer believes 'Without a doubt' | Cricket News - The Times of India
body: Virat Kohli completed 9,000 IPL runs on Monday during the match against Delhi Capitals, finishing the game with back-to-back sixes. His next target is the special milestone of 10,000 IPL runs, which remains far away for other current players, even those in the top five. Although Kohli is already 37, raising some questions about longevity, former New Zealand pacer Mitchell McClenaghan believes he can achieve the feat without any doubt. Mitchell McClenaghan praised Virat Kohli’s remarkable consistency after Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s emphatic nine-wicket victory over Delhi Capitals in their IPL 2026 clash. Watch Axar Patel shares big update on Mitchell Starc RCB’s dominant win at the Arun Jaitley Stadium on Monday was set up by a fiery new-ball spell from Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood, who dismantled Delhi’s batting line-up and bowled them out for just 75, the lowest total of the season. The chase was completed with ease, highlighting RCB’s all-round supremacy. Reflecting on Kohli’s sustained excellence in the tournament, McClenaghan highlighted the discipline and adaptability that have defined the veteran batter’s journey over the years, speaking on JioHotstar. “Virat Kohli has been incredibly consistent. Scoring 9000 IPL runs against the world’s best bowlers is no easy task. It's about hunger, discipline, and adapting every year, and that’s what brings the best out of him. He has been in the IPL since 2008, and the best part is that he didn’t start his career as an opener. He batted in the lower middle order, fought for his place, then moved to number three. Later, he opened with Chris Gayle. That changed his game completely. He will soon reach 10,000 runs, without a doubt. Kohli is the greatest batter the IPL has ever seen, not just for the runs, but for how he has evolved and dominated across different eras,” McClenaghan told JioStar. Here are the top 5 leading run-scorers in IPL:“Instead of reinventing, he has been doing the same stuff consistently, over and over again, and he is just getting better at it. The time off from international cricket is now helping him structure his time better throughout the year. He's getting enough time to work on fitness and stay fresh. He has enough experience and knows how to deal with pressure situations. It is just about being able to do what you have been doing. That means hitting those areas with the ball which trouble batters the most, and having control over swing, which we are seeing right now. That is why his impact has always been great with the new ball," he added. Upcoming matches of Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB):

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Iran after ceasefire with US: Holding ground in an uncertain pause - The Times of India
body: - News - World News - Middle East News - Iran after ceasefire with US: Holding ground in an uncertain pause Trending For weeks, the US–Israel war with Iran was measured in strikes, targets, and retaliation across multiple fronts. Now, under a fragile two‑week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan and announced on April 7, the metric has shifted. The immediate focus is no longer just on battlefield damage, but on whether the conflict has brought bigger fundamental changes and whether the ceasefire can hold.The truce brought a temporary halt to large‑scale US and Israeli strikes on Iran and opened a narrow diplomatic window. However, reports of renewed missile alerts and attacks across the Gulf almost immediately cast doubt on its durability. In broad terms, the emerging picture is mixed and unresolved: Tehran has absorbed significant military pressure, key strategic leverage remains in play, and the political and military apparatus continues to function despite high‑profile casualties. At the same time, the conflict has exacted heavy costs on economies, civilians, and the wider Middle East security environment. The ceasefire has not fully quieted the battlefield. Within hours of its announcement, missile alerts were reported in the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, Qatar and other Gulf states.Iran’s state and semi‑official media, cited by Reuters, reported that oil tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz were halted after what they described as an Israeli “ceasefire breach,” and that Tehran could withdraw from the agreement if fighting in Lebanon continued. Despite these losses, Tehran has swiftly appointed successors and maintained centralised decision‑making. This continuity complicates simplistic narratives about “regime collapse,” with the state’s institutions remaining functional even amid leadership attrition.Public messaging has diverged sharply: President Donald Trump declared that the campaign had achieved “regime change” and insisted negotiations were now occurring with “different people,” while Iranian officials framed the pause as a strategic victory that compelled adversaries to accept conditions without eroding core institutional control. Also, the conflict has also shown that US forces incurred losses and faced resistance from Iranian air defences.A US F-15E fighter jet was shot down over Iran during operations, marking one of the most significant aerial losses of the conflict. Both crew members ejected. One was rescued within hours, while the second remained on the ground for nearly two days before being extracted in a high-risk rescue mission involving multiple aircraft and special forces.The rescue operation itself came under fire, with conflicting accounts on aircraft losses. US-linked accounts said some aircraft were destroyed by their own forces after becoming unusable to prevent them from falling into Iranian hands. Iranian officials, however, claimed their air defences shot down multiple US aircraft involved in the mission.A US A-10 attack aircraft was also lost during the conflict, with the pilot ejecting and surviving. The US military has officially confirmed that 13 American service members were killed during the conflict, with over 350–370 personnel wounded, most of them sustaining non-fatal injuries and later returning to duty.Earlier in the war, casualties included troops killed in Iranian drone and missile strikes on US bases in the region, as well as additional deaths linked to operational incidents, including an aircraft crash.Iran has also repeatedly claimed it shot down several US drones, particularly MQ-9 Reapers, though the exact number of such losses has not been independently confirmed.Throughout the conflict, Iran has continued to launch drones and ballistic missiles against Israel, US bases, and Gulf Arab states, including Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Kuwait, and the United Arab Emirates. Even in the hours after the ceasefire announcement, Gulf states reported intercepting incoming projectiles, reflecting that Iranian long-range systems remain functional. End of Article Follow Us On Social Media

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Bihar BTSC lab assistant notification released for 1091 posts at btsc.bihar.gov.in; apply here - The Times of India
body: Bihar BTSC lab assistant recruitment 2026: In this regard, the Bihar Technical Service Commission (BTSC) has released the complete notification for the Recruitment of Laboratory Assistants with 1091 posts overall. This notification was released on April 6, 2026, and includes all information related to the eligibility criteria, application process, and other related things. Applications can be submitted starting April 6, 2026, till May 6, 2026. Students interested in getting government technical jobs in Bihar should read the notification thoroughly prior to submission. Important dates for BTSC lab assistant recruitment 2026BTSC Lab Assistant Vacancy 2026The vacancy list is quite varied across different technical branches. The largest number of vacancies are provided for Computer Science Engineering & Information Technology at 252 positions. There are 166 vacancies for Civil Engineering & Rural Engineering, 166 for Electrical Engineering, 166 for Mechanical Engineering, and 164 for Science. Combined, there are 146 posts for Electronics Engineering and Electronics & Communication Engineering. Other departments which have lesser posts include Textile Engineering (20), Automobile Engineering and Mechanical Diesel (4), Ceramics, Printing, and Chemical Engineering (2 each). The least allocation is found in Agriculture and Pharmacy, where only one post each is available. • Dirct link to check and download the official notification | Short Notice• Direct link to apply onlineEligibility Criteria & Age Limitation DefinedCandidates are required to complete Intermediate level education (10 + 2) in science stream under any recognized board. Besides this, candidates need to qualify either a Diploma/BTech/BE degree course in an engineering discipline from a recognized university/institution. As far as eligibility marks are concerned, general/OBC candidates need to score a minimum of 60% marks whereas SC/ST candidates should score minimum 55% marks. Age criteria set by the organization are that minimum age of candidate should be 21 years. Upper age limit of unreserved category candidates is 37 years whereas that of unreserved female/BC/EBC candidates is 40 years and that of SC/ST category candidates is 42 years. Application Fee & Selection Process ExplainedThe application fee will be Rs. 600 for general, backward caste, and Economically Weaker Section candidates, as well as those from other states besides Bihar. Candidates belonging to SC/ST communities having permanent address in Bihar and women candidates irrespective of categories will have to pay Rs. 150 only. Selection Process: It comprises computer-based online test, followed by evaluation of work experience or higher studies, verification of documents, and medical test. The written test shall carry weightage of 75% whereas work experience and higher qualification will carry 25%.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Auston Matthews and William Nylander urge Toronto Maple Leafs management to address roster weaknesses this NHL offseason urgently | NHL News - The Times of India
body: The Toronto Maple Leafs are already feeling the heat after a steep decline in the Atlantic Division. Now internal voices are starting to shape the offseason narrative. According to reports, team leaders Auston Matthews and William Nylander have taken the reins and made their expectations for management clear. This growing urgency reflects mounting pressure within theToronto Maple Leafslocker room ahead of a crucial offseason reset. Their message: If you want to compete in the National Hockey League, get better at roster construction. The elite talent is now on board, and the focus turns to assembling a more balanced, playoff-ready roster. Watch PLAYOFF HOCKEY TOMORROW 🚨 Ft. TSN's Darren Dreger | FN Barn Burner - April 17th, 2026 Auston Matthews and William Nylander outline the Toronto Maple Leafs' needs before next NHL season Toronto Maple Leafs forwards Auston Matthews and William Nylander have said they want the team to add physical forwards and mobile defensemen to the roster to compete next season. The priority is clear, Toronto needs more physical top-six forwards who can play at playoff intensity and protect star players. There is talent on this team, but not the sort of consistent physicality that can be a major factor in a game of pressure. On the defensive side of things, it’s all about puck movement and transition. Upgrades are needed in mobile defensemen who can help offensively and on the power play. Improving here will give Matthews and Nylander more opportunities to generate scoring chances. This philosophy is in line with the current NHL trends of speed, physicality and defensive agility that have led to successful teams. Matthews and Nylander are strong in the core, but now it’s time to get those complementary pieces. On a larger scale, the move is a sign of the increasing power players have over roster decisions around the league. Increasingly, the star players have a say in the direction of the team, especially when a team expects to contend for a championship. Toronto has a lot riding on this. Another mediocre season could lead to more structural changes. How the team addresses those issues in the offseason will likely determine whether it returns to contention or continues to struggle. The NHL offseason is coming up and all eyes are on how management responds to these internal calls.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: From Hormuz to household finance: How global oil shocks can filter into Indian homes - The Times of India
body: - News - Business News - India Business News - From Hormuz to household finance: How global oil shocks can filter into Indian homes Trending The Iran–US conflict has pushed global oil markets into turmoil, with tensions still simmering despite a two-week ceasefire window, since April 8, intended to enable negotiations. The peace talks amid the fragile truce have so far failed to yield any breakthrough, though diplomatic sources indicate preparations for a second round of talks are already under way. The standoff nevertheless has spilled into one of the world’s most critical energy chokepoints. The US began enforcing a naval blockade around the Strait of Hormuz, targeting vessels linked to Iranian oil exports and disrupting traffic through a narrow corridor that carries nearly 20 per cent of global crude supply.The US naval blockade, imposed this week after failed peace talks, has compounded earlier disruptions by directly targeting Iranian exports and turning back vessels moving through the Strait of Hormuz.However, in the most recent development on Friday, Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz “completely open” to commercial vessels for the duration of the Lebanon ceasefire, marking a temporary easing of tensions in the key global energy chokepoint. The result is a layered supply shock. Brent crude has swung past the $100 per barrel mark at multiple points during the crisis, reflecting not just immediate supply constraints but a sustained risk premium tied to prolonged instability in one of the world’s most critical oil corridors.For India, the developments are more than a distant geopolitical flashpoint. The country imports over 80 per cent of its crude oil requirement, with a significant share routed through the Gulf. Any disruption,whether a full blockade or even tighter enforcement,feeds directly into domestic costs.What begins as a military and strategic confrontation at sea quickly sets off an economic chain reaction: from crude oil to fuel prices, from fuel to inflation, from inflation to interest rates, and ultimately into household finances. Petrol, diesel, and LPG prices respond first. But the broader impact is felt through logistics. Higher diesel costs raise freight rates, which in turn increase the price of everything from vegetables to consumer goods.Within weeks of sustained price increases, these pressures begin to show up in headline inflation.However, the transmission into core inflation, excluding food and fuel, is less immediate and depends on how deeply cost pressures spread across sectors.This was explained by Vivek Iyer, Partner and Financial Services Risk Advisory Leader at Grant Thornton Bharat, talking to TOI, “Core inflation is a function of demand side shocks or supply side shocks. The RBI monetary policy is usually used to address the demand side shocks and the fiscal policy to address the supply side shocks. The geopolitical tensions will have an impact on headline inflation but don’t see an impact on core inflation as the domestic growth story for India continues to be strong.” That distinction matters. When inflation is imported through oil rather than generated by overheating demand, central banks tend to avoid aggressive rate hikes that could unnecessarily slow growth.As Vivek Iyer of Grant Thornton Bharat said, “We don’t expect the interest rate to be on a higher side, as in a globally slow economy and with growth for India being domestically driven, RBI will take a measured approach while keeping a close watch on how inflation expectations evolve.”Thus, the implication is clear: while rate cuts may be delayed, a sharp tightening cycle remains unlikely unless oil shocks begin to feed more persistently into core inflation. - Expected relief on EMIs will be delayed: Rate cuts that were earlier anticipated in the first half of the financial year could be pushed further out. - Borrowing costs not come down for longer: Even without fresh hikes, banks are likely to keep lending rates elevated in line with the RBI’s cautious stance. - The easing cycle could shift further into the year: Policy may prioritise inflation stability over growth support until oil-driven pressures show signs of easing. - Lending rates are likely to stay higher: Banks tend to price loans off policy expectations, not just current rates. - Borrowing costs will not come down: From home loans to personal credit, interest costs may not ease quickly. - Discretionary consumption could soften gradually: With EMIs and credit costs not coming down spending on non-essential goods may not go up and may even see softening if prices go up - Selling bonds to absorb excess liquidity - Increasing reserve requirements for banks - Actively managing surplus funds in the banking system Unlike interest rate changes, liquidity tightening works in the background. But as seen in earlier cycles, it can still push up borrowing costs and slow credit growth across the economy. That said, the Grant Thornton partner noted that while the RBI continues to monitor systemic liquidity given its influence on money market rates, this may not directly translate into tighter retail credit conditions. - Rate cuts are being delayed - Lending rates remain sticky - Liquidity is being managed more actively Home loan EMIs stay elevatedFloating-rate borrowers see limited benefit if rate cuts are pushed back, increasing total interest outgo over the life of the loan.Costlier consumer creditCar loans, personal loans, and credit card interest rates remain high, discouraging discretionary spending.Savings see partial upsideHigher deposit rates can benefit savers, but this often coincides with slower economic momentum, which can affect income growth. - Rising cost of living driven by fuel-led inflation - Elevated borrowing costs due to delayed monetary easing End of Article Follow Us On Social Media

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi wins heart with humble gesture for young fan - Watch | Cricket News - The Times of India
body: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is set to face the in-form Punjab Kings at their home ground in Mullanpur, arriving on the back of a brilliant century against Sunrisers Hyderabad. Despite the surge in confidence, he has remained grounded and humble. In a video shared by the Rajasthan Royals on social media, Sooryavanshi, who recently slammed a 35-ball century against the Sunrisers Hyderabad, was welcomed at the team hotel with a garland. However, he presented it to a young kid waiting with other fans to catch a glimpse of the Riyan Parag-led side. Match Preview: PBKS vs RR Punjab Kings’ opening duo Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya have been the most explosive pair in IPL 2026, scoring 323 runs at a blistering run rate of 14.9. Meanwhile, Rajasthan Royals’ openers Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, currently behind them, will be eager to respond, especially against a Punjab bowling attack that has struggled in the powerplay. Watch Virat Kohli inaugurates his childhood coach Rajkumar Sharma's new academy Rajasthan, after a strong start to the season, have lost three of their last four matches and need a turnaround. Their hopes rest heavily on the top order, while captain Riyan Parag will look for better contributions from himself and Shimron Hetmyer. Punjab Kings, unbeaten at their New Chandigarh home this season, come in with strong batting momentum but have concerns of their own, including poor powerplay bowling and sloppy fielding. The contest is likely to be defined by the battle between the two opening pairs, although Rajasthan will also rely on Jofra Archer and Nandre Burger to make early inroads and shift the game in their favour.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: ‘Rishi Kapoor was my ideal, now I play Ranbir Kapoor’s father in Love & War’: Ashwin Kaushal on landing Sanjay Leela Bhansali’s film | - The Times of India
body: Ranbir Kapoor, Alia Bhatt and Vicky Kaushal-starrer Love & War has been steadily building buzz, and a new behind-the-scenes revelation has now added to the intrigue. Senior actor Ashwin Kaushal, who plays Ranbir Kapoor’s father in the film, has shared how the role came his way. ‘Was initially considered for another role’ In an interview with Siddharth Kannan, Ashwin Kaushal revealed that his casting wasn’t part of the original plan. The actor shared that filmmaker Sanjay Leela Bhansali had first called him in for a different part. “In February last year, Sanjay Leela Bhansali wanted to meet me. Their set was being put up in Film City. I went there and met him for the first time. He told me there were one or two sequences in the film — that was for another role. The next day, I got a call from his office saying that after observing me, he had something else in mind. That’s when I was told I would be playing Ranbir Kapoor’s father,” he said. Watch 'Raha Is My Dal Chawal’: Co-Star Reveals Ranbir Kapoor’s Adorable Fatherly Side! ‘He’s extremely grounded’ Ashwin also spoke about his experience of working with Ranbir Kapoor, highlighting the actor’s humility despite his stardom. Calling it a full-circle moment, he referenced his admiration for Rishi Kapoor. “Rishi Kapoor was my ideal, almost like a father figure to me. And now I got to play Ranbir’s father — destiny works in strange ways,” he said. He added, “Ranbir is extremely well-mannered. There’s absolutely no attitude. He met me with so much warmth and even knew that I had worked with his father in Damini. He was very cooperative.” Big release locked for 2027 Meanwhile, Love & War is shaping up to be one of Bhansali’s biggest films to date. The filmmaker recently locked January 21, 2027, as the release date, aligning it with the Republic Day weekend. The film is also said to be mounted on a massive scale. Reports suggest that large-scale aerial action sequences and extensive VFX work are among the key reasons behind its prolonged production timeline, making it one of Bhansali’s most ambitious ventures yet.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Inside ‘Satguru Sharan’: Exploring Saif Ali Khan and Kareena Kapoor Khan’s Rs 100 crore Bandra home | Hindi Movie News - The Times of India
body: Tucked away in one of Mumbai’s most sought-after neighbourhoods, ‘Satguru Sharan’ stands as the elegant residence of Saif Ali Khan and Kareena Kapoor Khan. Home to their sons Taimur and Jeh, this expansive four-floor apartment reflects a seamless blend of old-world charm and modern sophistication, making it one of Bollywood’s most talked-about celebrity homes. Saif Ali Khan and Kareena Kapoor's forever home Before moving into ‘Satguru Sharan’, the couple spent over a decade in their Bandra apartment at ‘Fortune Heights’. While their previous residence offered a cosy yet contemporary vibe with warm lighting and elegant décor, the arrival of their second son prompted the need for a more spacious and private setting. In 2021, the family transitioned to their new home, choosing scale, comfort, and enhanced security as priorities for this next chapter. Watch Saif Ali Khan Revisits Knife Attack Horror, Admits Thought of Being Bedridden Still Scares Him A property that turned heads According to Financial Express, the property was originally purchased by Saif in 2012. Over the years, it has seen a significant rise in value and is now estimated to be worth over Rs 100 crore. The residence gained widespread attention in early 2025 following a shocking break-in incident, during which Saif sustained injuries and was rushed for medical treatment. While deeply unsettling, the episode brought unprecedented focus to the otherwise private address. See More: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ box office collection day 19: Ranveer Singh, Rakesh Bedi, Sanjay Dutt, Sara Arjun's film eyes Rs 1,630 cr mark worldwidePatudi house's elegant interiors Designed by interior expert Darshini Shah, the home carries forward the warmth of their previous space but on a much grander scale. The interiors are defined by soft pastel tones, rich wooden textures, and thoughtfully curated décor elements. The living area stands out with its cream-toned seating, a striking Turkish rug, and a mix of classic and contemporary aesthetics. Victorian-style black-and-white chequered flooring adds a timeless touch to parts of the home. Saif’s passion for reading finds expression in a bespoke in-house library, complete with antique-style shelves and ambient lighting. Kareena’s walk-in wardrobe, often seen on her social media, exudes glamour with mirrored panels, designer lighting, and neatly arranged luxury collections. Meanwhile, the children’s rooms bring a playful contrast, featuring pastel hues and jungle-themed designs that add a lively charm. Saif Ali Khna's home is a place where luxury meets comfort Spread across multiple levels, the residence includes five bedrooms, a gym, a music room, and several open terraces. One of its standout features is the private terrace pool, a favourite family spot, especially for relaxed weekends and intimate gatherings. ‘Satguru Sharan’ isn’t just a home; it’s a reflection of a lifestyle that balances heritage, comfort, and modern luxury, perfectly mirroring the personalities of its celebrated residents.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: More women in jobs: How pro-active are they in financial and tax planning? - The Times of India
body: - News - Business News - India Business News - More women in jobs: How pro-active are they in financial and tax planning? Trending As women continue to expand their presence in the corporate workforce, their role in managing money is evolving alongside it. In a space traditionally dominated by men, women are increasingly stepping in—investing, evaluating options and engaging with financial advice. Financial decision-making, once seen through a gendered lens, is now emerging as a practical necessity rather than a defined role.From mutual funds and equities to safer instruments, participation is broadening. But even as involvement grows, the degree of independent control—particularly in areas like tax planning—remains uneven, suggesting a transition that is still under way.So, let’s dive deep into how much women get involved in investment and tax planning. Improving financial outcomes will require stronger financial literacy, better access to professional advice, and greater confidence in long-term investing, enabling women to play a more active role in building and managing their financial portfolios. Sanaa Zia Khan, director at Centricity Overseas Financial Distribution Pvt. Ltd. and a founding member of INVICTUS, Centricity WealthTech - Income Classification: Unlike a salary, freelance income is considered and reported under the head "Profits and Gains from Business or Profession." - ITR Filing: You must file ITR-3 or ITR-4 (Presumptive taxation) instead of the standard ITR-1. - Tax Regimes: The New Tax Regime is the default. Unlike reporting salary income where switching regimes is easy, taxpayers with business and professional income need to file Form 10-IEA to opt for the old tax regime. - TDS Awareness: Clients typically deduct 10% TDS under Section 194J on payments exceeding Rs. 50,000 for professional services. Anandan also detailed some common mistakes to be aware of for easy and accurate tax filing like being aware of last dates and the intricacies around availing the presumptive benefit or some deduction benefits women need to be aware about. Meanwhile, among survey respondents, only a small proportion said they actively plan their taxes through the year, while the majority either plan occasionally or assess their tax liability only at the time of filing returns.This pattern is visible across responses. This pattern is visible across responses. Prachi Kumari, a PR professional in her 20s, and Anjali Yadav, a communication specialist in her 20s, both of whom manage their investments independently, were among those who said they assess taxes only at the time of ITR filing.Even among those who engage more actively, tax planning is often periodic rather than continuous. Several respondents (out of 30) said they plan “sometimes”, while a smaller segment reported planning annually with a clearer view of potential liabilities.For many salaried individuals, tax-saving remains limited to standard deductions or last-minute investments, rather than a structured, year-round strategy. Experts noted that this can lead to missed opportunities for optimising returns and building long-term wealth. - Limited early exposure to financial decision-making: A key factor is limited early exposure to financial decision-making. Several respondents cited lack of financial knowledge as a primary barrier, often leading to reliance on family members or advisors. One respondent (talking anonymously) said she has “grown accustomed to relying on my dad” for financial decisions, reflecting how these patterns can carry across generations. - Household dynamics: Household dynamics also continue to play a role. While many women said they actively contribute to discussions, decision-making is still, in some cases, delegated. For instance, a professor in her 50s, speaking anonymously, said she relies on family or advisors for managing investments and assesses taxes only at the time of filing, reflecting a broader trend of partial involvement rather than full ownership. - Time constraints and competing priorities: Respondents across age groups pointed to busy work schedules, caregiving responsibilities and limited bandwidth as reasons for not managing investments or tax planning more proactively. Behavioural factors such as fear of risk and limited disposable income also emerged strongly. Several participants said concerns about potential losses or insufficient funds hold them back from exploring market-linked instruments or more sophisticated strategies. - Treating tax planning as compliance rather than strategy: Tax planning itself is often viewed as a compliance exercise rather than a strategic tool. Many respondents admitted to assessing taxes only at the time of filing or “sometimes” during the year, reinforcing the tendency to approach it reactively rather than as part of long-term financial planning. End of Article Follow Us On Social Media

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Ipl Purple Cap: IPL 2026 Purple Cap: Bhuvneshwar Kumar surges ahead after record-breaking spell | Cricket News - The Times of India
body: NEW DELHI: Veteran seamer Bhuvneshwar Kumar delivered a performance for the ages, not only powering Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a crushing win over Delhi Capitals but also storming to the top of the Purple Cap standings in IPL 2026. His sensational spell of 3 for 5 in three overs marked his 20th three-wicket haul in the IPL, making him only the second pacer after Jasprit Bumrah to achieve the milestone. With this effort, Bhuvneshwar took his tally to 14 wickets from eight matches, drawing level with Chennai Super Kings’ Anshul Kamboj and Sunrisers Hyderabad’s Eshan Malinga. However, his superior economy rate of 7.61 — compared to Kamboj’s 8.56 and Malinga’s 9.44 — helped him snatch the Purple Cap. Watch IPL 2026: LSG's Justin Langer explains where they lost vs KKR Rajasthan Royals’ Jofra Archer and Lucknow Super Giants’ Prince Yadav follow closely with 13 wickets each. Latest IPL 2026 Purple Cap standings Powerplay carnage sets up emphatic winThe match itself was defined by a brutal new-ball spell as Bhuvneshwar and Josh Hazlewood combined to dismantle DC’s batting line-up. The hosts were left reeling at 13 for 6 in the powerplay, with Bhuvneshwar striking early by castling debutant Sahil Parakh and later removing Tristan Stubbs and skipper Axar Patel. Hazlewood complemented him perfectly, returning with figures of 4 for 12 as DC collapsed to 75 in 16.3 overs. Reflecting on his performance, Bhuvneshwar credited the conditions and early swing. “When the ball swings and you get a few wickets upfront, it makes it easier,” he said, adding that the team was slightly surprised by the assistance on offer but made the most of it. He also joked about not pushing for an extra over due to fatigue, saying he was “happy with three overs” considering the heat. Chasing a modest target, RCB completed the job with ease in just 6.3 overs. Virat Kohli and Devdutt Padikkal ensured there were no hiccups, with Kohli sealing the win in style with consecutive sixes.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Assam HS Class 12th result 2026 to be released tomorrow: Here's how you can download scorecards from DigiLocker - The Times of India
body: Assam HS result 2026: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has confirmed that the Assam Higher Secondary (HS) or Class 12th results will be declared tomorrow, April 28, 2026. Approximately, over 3.3 lakh students had taken the exam. The confirmation comes after the Assam State School Education Board dismissed a widely circulated fake notice that had earlier suggested an April 24 release. The Assam HS examinations were conducted between February 13 and March 17, with practicals held earlier in the year. Alongside official result portals, students can access their digital scorecards through DigiLocker, the government-backed platform designed for secure document storage. In 2025, results were announced on April 30, while 2024 saw an early May release. The board has been steadily advancing the schedule to better align with undergraduate admission cycles across India. Last year’s overall pass percentage crossed 88 per cent, with Science stream students recording the highest success rate. This year’s performance trends will be closely monitored, particularly as competition for university seats intensifies. Assam HS result 2026: Steps to download scorecards Candidates can follow the steps elucidated here to download the Assam HS results 2026 from the official website: - Visit the official result portal, asseb.in and ahsec.assam.gov.in. - Click on the link for “Assam HS Result 2026” on the homepage - Enter your roll number and other required credentials - Submit the details - Your result will appear on the screen - Download the scorecard and take a printout for future use Assam HS Class 12th result 2026: Steps to download from DigiLocker Aspirants are advised to follow the steps elucidated here to download the Assam HS Class 12th result 2026 from the official website: - Go to the DigiLocker website or open the mobile app - Sign in using your registered mobile number or Aadhaar credentials - If new, complete the registration process - Navigate to the ‘Education’ section - Select Assam HS Result 2026 from the list - Enter required details such as roll number - Download the digital marksheet for future use Aspirants are advised to stay tuned to the official website to get the complete details of the Assam HS result 2026.

headline: IPL 2026: Why is Shashank Singh not playing against Rajasthan Royals in New Chandigarh? | Cricket News - The Times of India
body: Shashank Singh was not picked for the Punjab Kings' clash against the Rajasthan Royals at the Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur, New Chandigarh. The two sides are facing off in Match 40 of the season in what is a key contest between two top-four teams. At the toss, Riyan Parag won the coin flip and opted to field first for Rajasthan Royals. Watch Axar Patel shares big update on Mitchell Starc Shreyas Iyer on Shashank’s Absence Punjab Kings skipper Shreyas Iyer confirmed that Shashank Singh’s absence was a forced change, without revealing the exact reason he said that, “We would have loved to bowl first as well. The wicket was covered for the last few days because of the weather. All of us are proactive, irrespective of whether we are bowling first or second. We have one forced change, Suryansh comes in place of Shashank. Also, Lockie comes in place of Xavier.” Team Updates: PBKS vs RR Rajasthan Royals Changes:Out: Shimron Hetmyer, Ravi Bishnoi In: Dasun Shanaka, Yash Raj Punia Punjab Kings Changes:Out: Shashank Singh (injury), Xavier Bartlett In: Suryansh Shedge, Lockie FergusonPlaying XIs for Punjab Kings and Rajasthan Royals Punjab Kings (Playing XI): Prabhsimran Singh(w), Priyansh Arya, Shreyas Iyer(c), Cooper Connolly, Suryansh Shedge, Nehal Wadhera, Marcus Stoinis, Marco Jansen, Lockie Ferguson, Arshdeep Singh, Yuzvendra Chahal Rajasthan Royals (Playing XI): Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Yashasvi Jaiswal, Dhruv Jurel(w), Riyan Parag(c), Donovan Ferreira, Ravindra Jadeja, Dasun Shanaka, Jofra Archer, Nandre Burger, Brijesh Sharma, Yash Raj Punja Impact subs for Punjab Kings and Rajasthan Royals Rajasthan Royals Impact subs: Ravi Bishnoi, Ravi Singh, Tushar Deshpande, Shubham Dubey, Shimron Hetmyer Punjab Kings Impact Subs: Vishnu Vinod, Vijaykumar Vyshak, Xavier Bartlett, Harpreet Brar, Musheer Khan

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Aamir Khan tears up watching Junaid Khan's acting, praises Sai Pallavi at Ek Din event: 'Best actress in our country today' | Hindi Movie News - The Times of India
body: Aamir Khan was seen getting emotional at a special event for his upcoming production Ek Din in the city on Sunday. The actor attended the event along with his son Junaid Khan and co-star Sai Pallavi. As performances unfolded at the event, Aamir appeared visibly moved and was seen breaking down in tears. The emotional moment caught everyone’s attention, with the actor later also taking to the stage to croon a song from the film. Watch Aamir Khan Gets Emotional At Son Junaid’s Ek Din Event, Heartwarming Family Moment Goes Viral During the event, Aamir didn’t hold back in praising Sai Pallavi, calling her “the best actress we have in our country today.” He also spoke about his son Junaid in a lighter vein, adding with a smile, “Junaid ne bhi acha khasa kaam kiya hai (Junaid has also done quite a good job).” About Ek Din Ek Din is a romantic drama directed by Sunil Pandey and produced by Mansoor Khan, Aamir Khan, and Aparna Purohit under Aamir Khan Productions. The new trailer of the upcoming film ‘Ek Din’, which stars Sai Pallavi and Junaid Khan in the lead, was unveiled on Thursday. The trailer promises a love story peppered with hope and magic. In the trailer, Junaid Khan portrays a shy and a different guy, who develops feelings for Sai Pallavi’s character, who is a cheerful and full-of-life girl. While they both work in the same office, Junaid struggles to gather the confidence to talk to her. However, a twist unfolds in Japan when Sai meets with an accident and suffers from Transient Global Amnesia (TGA), remembering only Junaid, who saved her life. Poll Which actor are you more excited to see in Ek Din? The film marks Junaid Khan’s 2nd big screen outing after Loveyapa, and Sai Pallavi’s Bollywood debut. The film is set to release in theaters on May 1, 2026. On the work front, Aamir was last seen in Sitaare Zameen Par, a spiritual successor in tone to Taare Zameen Par. The film marked his return to acting after a break following the failure of Laal Singh Chaddha. See More: Aamir Khan tears up at son Junaid Khan and Sai Pallavi’s ‘Ek Din Ki Mehfil’ event: 5 times the actor got emotional in public

headline: Ipl 2026 Schedule: IPL 2026 schedule this week (April 6 to April 12): Full list of matches, dates, timings, venues and results | Cricket News - The Times of India
body: IPL 2026 schedule this week (6-12 April): After nine thrilling matches last week, a trend is beginning to emerge on which teams are on a roll and which are still struggling to settle into the new season. So far, 15 matches have been played in the new season of the IPL, and four teams have already set the early marker in the race for the playoffs. With four wins in four matches, Rajasthan Royals sit at the top of the points table. Punjab Kings are at second after two wins and a no result due to rain. They are followed by defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru, Delhi Capitals and Lucknow Super Giants, who have two wins each so far. Meanwhile, Chennai Super Kings and Kolkata Knight Riders are two winless teams in IPL 2026. Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel. SUBSCRIBE NOW!With a win each, Sunrisers Hyderabad, Mumbai Indians, and Gujarat Titans have opened their account in the standings and will look to challenge the top four in coming fixtures. The ongoing IPL season has already witnessed multiple weather disruptions across the country. The KKR vs PBKS clash in Kolkata was washed out, while the RR vs MI encounter had to be curtailed to just 11 overs per side in which Rajasthan Royals outclassed Mumbai Indians by 27 runs. And on Wednesday, GT held their nerves to beat hosts DC by one run in a last-ball thriller. KKR lost their 3rd match in a row after Mukul Choudhary pulled off a record breaking chase for LSG in the last-ball thriller. Rajasthan Royals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru, face off in Guwahati on Friday. The weekend double-headers will see Punjab Kings take on Sunrisers Hyderabad and Chennai Super Kings face Delhi Capitals on Saturday, followed by Lucknow Super Giants vs Gujarat Titans and Mumbai Indians vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday. There is likely to be plenty of shuffling in the points table this week. Full schedule of IPL 2026 this week (6-12 April): Matches, dates, timings, venues & results

headline: Has Dianna Russini’s career taken a long term hit from the alleged affair while Mike Vrabel remains secure with the New England Patriots? | NFL News - The Times of India
body: Mike Vrabel and former New York Times’ reporter and insider, Dianna Russini’s affair rumors have refused to die down and seems to have only gotten worse. As Dianna Russini has now stepped down from her role at The Athletic, she faces a pretty uncertain future in the league. Dianna Russini’s career seems to be at risk as Mike Vrabel’s job remains safe A few hours ago, FOX Sports’ Bobby Burack weighed in on Dianna Russini’s career as it seems to be taking a turn for the worse with each passing day while Mike Vrabel’s career remains stable. FOX Sports’ Bobby Burack noted in a recent report, “A television network is not going to hire a reporter who resigned from The New York Times after reportedly asking her to provide proof that her relationship with Vrabel was appropriate. If she eventually returns to media, it will almost certainly be exclusively online, where a social following is important.” FOX Sports’ Bobby Burack added, “Sources are likely to be less inclined to work with her going forward, given her reputation and lack of a mainstream platform. If she cannot offer insight, viewers will have little reason to consume her content.” FOX Sports’ Bobby Burack continued, “Likewise, Russini would have a hard time convincing readers to pay for her content on a platform like Substack.” Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini stayed distant from their scandalous 2020 pictures when they were spotted kissing Ever since 43 year old Dianna Russini stepped down from her prestigious role at The Athletic, she seems to have disappeared from the public eye. A few days ago, as many of her old posts on X were being dug up and being linked to the alleged affair drama between her and Mike Vrabel, Dianna Russini promptly deleted her account. Just hours after she permanently deleted her account on X, Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel’s scandalous pictures from 2020 were leaked by Page Six. The pictures showed them kissing as they were seated close at a high end bar in New York City. Neither Dianna Russini nor Mike Vrabel has clarified about the 2020 pictures as they try to remain out of the spotlight.

headline: JNVST Class 9th result for Lateral Entry released at navodaya.gov.in: Direct link to download scorecards here - The Times of India
body: The Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti (NVS) has officially declared the Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya Selection Test (JNVST) Class 9 Result 2026, on the official website, cbseit.in. Aspirants who have taken the exam can visit the official website to download the scorecards. The admission test for JNVs in Class 9 was held on February 7, 2026, across different centers throughout the country. Aspirants can access their results via roll number and date of birth login on the online portal. The result has been released in the form of a scorecard which includes important details like marks scored, selection status, and personal data of aspirants. Furthermore, NVS has published the selection list for each JNV separately. Aspirants need to go through their results very carefully to ensure that there is no mistake regarding their names, roll numbers, categories, districts, and allotted schools. JNVST Class 9 Result 2026: Steps to download To access the result, candidates should follow these steps: - Go to the official website: navodaya.gov.in - Click on the link titled “JNVST Class 9 Result 2026” - Enter roll number and date of birth - Submit the details to view the result - Download and retain the scorecard for future use Alternatively, candidates can click on the link provided here to download the JNVST Class 9th result 2026. Admission based on merit; Verification mandatory The procedure of admissions for Class 9 in Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas is purely meritocratic. The candidates have been selected on the basis of their scores in the entrance examination, provided that there are seats available for them in their particular regions. Now, the selected candidates will have to appear before the assigned JNVs for documentation purposes. It must be noted that non-presentation of authentic documents will lead to disqualification from admissions. JNVST Class 9 admissions 2026: Documents required for verification Candidates must present key documents during the verification process, including a birth certificate, residence or domicile certificate, previous class marksheet, Aadhaar card, and a category certificate, if applicable.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: Major setback: Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin rocket failure could delay NASA’s moon landing plans | - The Times of India
body: The recent failure of a Blue Origin mission has raised fresh questions about the timeline of NASA’s Artemis programme, even though the space agency was not directly involved in the launch. The company’s heavy-lift New Glenn rocket lifted off successfully but failed to place a commercial satellite into the correct orbit, ultimately leading to the loss of the payload. While this does not immediately derail NASA’s Moon landing ambitions, it introduces new uncertainties into an already complex and tightly scheduled programme aiming to return humans to the lunar surface later this decade. What went wrong during Blue Origin’s New Glenn mission The New Glenn launch initially appeared flawless. The rocket lifted off from Cape Canaveral and its booster stage executed a successful landing on a barge in the Atlantic Ocean. However, the mission’s primary objective, deploying a communications satellite built by AST SpaceMobile, was not achieved. Post-launch data revealed that the satellite had been inserted into an orbit that was too low to sustain operations. The company later confirmed the satellite was effectively lost. Such failures typically point to issues during the upper-stage burn phase, where precise velocity and altitude are critical. Although investigations are ongoing under the supervision of the Federal Aviation Administration, the rocket has been grounded pending further review. Why this matters for NASA’s Artemis programme Although NASA was not involved in this particular mission, the implications extend to its Artemis programme. Blue Origin has been contracted as one of the providers to develop a human landing system capable of transporting astronauts from lunar orbit to the Moon’s surface. The company’s lander is expected to rely in part on New Glenn for future missions. Any delays in certifying or returning the rocket to flight could therefore slow the development and testing of this lander. Artemis missions are highly interdependent, with spacecraft, launch systems and landing vehicles all needing to operate in coordination. A delay in one component can ripple across the entire schedule. Artemis timeline where Blue Origin fits NASA’s roadmap currently places the first crewed Moon landing of the Artemis era in the latter part of the decade. The earlier mission, Artemis III, is expected to rely primarily on systems developed by SpaceX, while Blue Origin’s lander is positioned to support subsequent missions and long term lunar operations. This means the recent failure does not directly threaten the immediate landing attempt. However, it does affect the broader timeline and NASA’s long term goal of establishing a sustained human presence on the Moon. The agency’s strategy relies on multiple commercial partners to reduce risk and improve flexibility, an approach that becomes more fragile when one partner encounters setbacks. The risk of losing redundancy One of the key reasons NASA selected multiple providers, including SpaceX and Blue Origin, was to avoid dependence on a single company. This redundancy is crucial in complex programmes where delays are common. If Blue Origin’s development timeline slips, NASA may become more reliant on a single provider. This increases overall programme risk. Should delays arise elsewhere, whether in launch systems, spacecraft readiness or spacesuit development, the absence of a fully ready second provider could make it harder to maintain the planned schedule. Investigation and the road ahead Failures of this nature typically trigger extensive investigations, including root cause analysis, hardware redesign and additional testing. The involvement of the Federal Aviation Administration means that New Glenn will remain grounded until safety and reliability concerns are addressed. For Blue Origin, this represents both a technical and reputational challenge. For NASA, it underscores the difficulty of executing one of the most ambitious space exploration programmes since Apollo. While the Artemis Moon landing is still on track in principle, maintaining that timeline will depend on how quickly Blue Origin resolves the issues and resumes progress. As NASA continues to push towards a return to the Moon, the success of Artemis will depend on the collective reliability of the ecosystem supporting it, rather than any single mission. Blue Origin’s next steps will therefore be closely watched by the industry and within the programme as it seeks to define the future of human spaceflight.

author: Trisha Mahajan
headline: "Behen Darr Gayi!": Fans relive ‘Bhagam Bhag’ era after watching Akshay Kumar's 'Bhooth Bangla' trailer | Hindi Movie News - The Times of India
body: Filmmaker Priyadarshan's upcoming horror-comedy 'Bhooth Bangla' has finally dropped, and it has left audiences excited. Actor Akshay Kumar is back with a bang and his style, on-screen presence, dialogue delivery, is getting lots of praise from the cinelovers. The trailer blends spooky moments with classic situational humour, reminding fans of the duo’s popular films from the 2000s. Early reactions suggest the film could be a fun entertainer. About 'Bhooth Bangla' trailer The trailer is unveiled with the caption, "Welcome to Bhooth Bangla. Yahan na toh log normal hain... Aur upar se Bangla bhi paranormal hai. Enter at your own risk." It follows London-returned Arjun Acharya, played by Akshay Kumar, who arrives in the town of Mangalpur for his sister’s wedding. He soon finds himself surrounded by terrified locals, strange ghostly events, and plenty of chaos. Watch Rajpal Yadav on his role in Bhoot Bangla, Asrani and working with Akshay Kumar Netizens’ reacts to 'Bhagam Bhag' reference The trailer has received a lot of love online but most viewers were impressed by the 'Bhagam Bhag' reference in the trailer where Akshay Kumar’s iconic dialogue, “Behen dar gai, behen dar gai,” has struck a chord and tickled the fans' funny bones, instantly reminding them of the comedy classic. 'Bhagam Bhag' This 2006 comedy entertainer flick directed by Priyadarshan and written by Neeraj Vora was one of Kumar's best film in his career. The film starred Govinda, Paresh Rawal along side Akshay Kumar. Iconic names like Lara Dutta, Rajpal Yadav, Jackie Shroff, Arbaaz Khan, Shakti Kapoor, Manoj Joshi, Razak Khan and late Asrani were seen in the key roles. Priyadarshan and Akshay Kumar reunion Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan are teaming up again after a long gap. Their earlier collaborations delivered some of Bollywood’s most loved comedies. The trailer suggests they are bringing back that signature style of situational humour mixed with light horror elements. Paresh Rawal and Rajpal Yadav are also part of the cast and it is all exciting for the fans. Story and release plans for 'Bhooth Bangla' 'Bhooth Bangla' is a fantasy horror-comedy directed by Priyadarshan. It is produced by Akshay Kumar, Ekta Kapoor and Shobha Kapoor. The film is scheduled to release in theatres on 17 April 2026. The trailer has built decent curiosity by promising both scares and laughs in equal measure. 'Bhooth Bangla'’s trailer has brought back memories of the golden era of Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan comedies and the excitement levels are high for all the right reasons. Watch Rajpal Yadav promotes ‘Bhoot Bangla’ with Akshay
